html:
[201]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 又越人某，二十余岁而亡 - Narrative opening introducing a character from Yue (越人) who died before the age of thirty; '又' suggests this is another story or case.
  • 逾年 - Means 'after a year,' indicating the passage of time since the person’s death.
  • 家婢 - Refers to a female servant or maid in the household.
  • 据客位坐，语操南音 - Describes the ghost taking a seat in the guest position and speaking with a southern accent; '客位' is the seat reserved for guests; '南音' refers to the dialect or accent of the southern region.
  • 大哥好心，欲令五弟归吾榇 - '大哥' refers to the deceased’s older brother; '五弟' refers to the deceased; '归吾榇' means 'return my coffin.'
  • 但五弟非人，不可托，不如附粮艘为便 - The deceased is no longer human and cannot be entrusted with the coffin; '附粮艘' suggests attaching the coffin to a grain boat for transport.
  • 此事仅夫妇有私议，外人无知者 - Indicates that the matter is only discussed privately between the husband and wife, and others are unaware of it.
  • 每闻土神报大哥作好事，则吾欢欣踊跃 - '土神' refers to local land deities or earth gods who report on the deeds of the living; the ghost is happy when the earth god reports that the older brother (大哥) has done good deeds.
  • 报坏事，则吾寝食不安 - If the earth god reports bad deeds, the ghost is disturbed in his sleep and meals, indicating a moral or supernatural consequence.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 越人 - Refers to someone from the ancient state of Yue (越), located in what is now Zhejiang Province; associated with the southern regions and a distinct cultural and dialectal identity.
  • 城隍神 - The city god or guardian spirit of a city or town; believed to oversee the moral conduct of the people and punish the wicked.
  • 土神 - The earth god or land god; associated with local spirits that protect the land and report on the behavior of people living in the area.
  • 阴司 - The underworld or the realm of the dead; a common term in Chinese religious and supernatural literature.
  • 吏 - A low-level official or functionary in the afterlife, similar to a bureaucratic role in the human world.
  • 职司考察 - '职司' means 'duty' or 'responsibility'; '考察' means 'to examine' or 'to investigate'; together, this refers to the ghost’s role in overseeing the moral conduct of the living.
  • 附粮艘 - A practical but supernatural suggestion to attach the coffin to a grain boat for transport, implying that the ghost is guiding the process from the afterlife.

Proper Nouns

  • 越人 - A geographical and cultural designation referring to people from the ancient state of Yue.
  • 城隍神 - The city god, a common title in Chinese religious and supernatural texts.
  • 土神 - The earth god or land god, a common figure in Chinese folk religion.
  • 阴司 - A term for the underworld or afterlife, used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 吏 - A general term for a low-level official or functionary, used here in the context of the afterlife bureaucracy.

[202]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 阴功 - Refers to good deeds that benefit others, often associated with religious or moral teachings.
  • 积德荫子孙 - The idea that good deeds benefit future generations, a common theme in Confucian and Taoist thought.
  • 天道无私 - The concept that heaven is impartial and just, a fundamental belief in Chinese philosophy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 神灵 - Refers to spirits or deities, often invoked in prayers or rituals.
  • 京师 - Refers to the capital city, historically significant in Chinese history.
  • 优伶 - Refers to actors or performers, often associated with entertainment and social gatherings.

Proper Nouns

  • 嫂 - Sister-in-law
  • 大哥 - Elder brother
  • 叔 - Uncle
  • 京师 - The capital city
  • 优伶 - Actors or performers
  • 千金 - A thousand taels of silver, a significant amount of money
  • 五弟 - Fifth younger brother

[203]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 郁郁 - Describes a state of depression or dissatisfaction, often used in classical literature.
  • 不通不通 - Means 'not understanding,' repeated for emphasis.
  • 人之所以得生者，气血耳 - Means 'the reason why people can be alive is due to vital energy and blood,' central concepts in traditional Chinese medicine and philosophy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钱塘 - An ancient name for Hangzhou, a city in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 童子试 - A traditional Chinese examination for young scholars, often taken by boys in their early teens.
  • 闻人孝廉 - A title given to scholars who passed the examination for their filial piety and integrity, indicating a respected position in society.
  • 神主 - Refers to the spirit tablet or ancestral tablet used in ancestor worship.
  • 家资 - Family assets or property.
  • 妻子 - Wife and children.
  • 气血 - Vital energy and blood, central concepts in traditional Chinese medicine and philosophy.

Proper Nouns

  • 沈咸 - The name of the deceased.
  • 闻人孝廉 - The name of the respected scholar.
  • 神主 - The spirit tablet.
  • 家资 - Family assets.
  • 妻子 - Wife and children.
  • 气血 - Vital energy and blood.

[204]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此身气绝血凝，何以能生？ - Rhetorical question meaning 'With my body's breath cut off and blood congealed, how could I possibly live?'
  • 我闻死则魂入阴司，阁下何不一行？ - Statement followed by a question: 'I have heard that after death, the soul enters the underworld; why don't you go there?'
  • 阴司何在？ - Direct question meaning 'Where is the underworld?'
  • 我自病革后，魂与体离，飘飘然无所归，只闻妻哭过悲，欲前慰之，不觉合而为一，然支持甚苦，刻刻欲分，想不能久住矣。 - Narrative describing the state of the soul: 'Since my illness worsened, my soul has separated from my body, wandering aimlessly without a place to go. I only heard my wife weeping in sorrow, wanting to comfort her, and unconsciously merged back into one, but the strain is unbearable, and I am constantly on the verge of separating again. I suppose I cannot stay for long.'
  • 鬼有所归，乃不为厉。 - Classical saying meaning 'If a ghost has a place to return to, it will not become malevolent.'
  • 唯。 - Single character often used to affirm or conclude a statement, here meaning 'Indeed.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 阴司 - Refers to the underworld or the realm of the dead in Chinese cosmology.
  • 魂 - The soul or spirit in Chinese philosophy.
  • 阴司何在？ - This question reflects the cultural belief in the existence of an afterlife and the underworld.
  • 鬼有所归，乃不为厉。 - This saying is rooted in traditional Chinese beliefs about the afterlife and the importance of proper burial and remembrance to prevent ghosts from becoming malevolent.

Proper Nouns

  • 孝廉 - A title for a scholar-official in the Han dynasty, often used to refer to a person of high moral and scholarly standing.
  • 阴司 - The underworld or the realm of the dead.
  • 魂 - The soul or spirit.
  • 厉 - A malevolent ghost or spirit.

[205]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 國家設官分職 - Formal phrase meaning 'the state appoints officials and assigns duties.'
  • 佐朝廷以安養百姓者也 - Means 'to assist the court in ensuring the well-being of the people.'
  • 然者 - Term used to introduce an exception or a contrasting case.
  • 莫如前朝仕宦之强争 - Means 'nothing is like the fierce competition among officials in the previous dynasty.'
  • 以魚肉鄉愚為勝 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to treat the ignorant villagers as fish and meat, i.e., to oppress them.'
  • 寧使官家放火，不許百姓點燈之謠 - Proverb meaning 'rather let the government set fire, than allow the people to light lamps,' indicating severe oppression.
  • 雷令風行 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to act with the speed and force of thunder and wind, i.e., to enforce orders strictly.'
  • 極一時之盛 - Means 'to reach the peak of prosperity in one's time.'
  • 應綿祖德報君恩 - Means 'to continue the virtue of ancestors and repay the grace of the emperor.'
  • 以長享富貴耳 - Means 'to enjoy long-lasting wealth and honor.'
  • 成群結夥 - Means 'to gather in groups.'
  • 佔據於茶坊酒肆 - Means 'to occupy tea houses and taverns.'
  • 攪擾不休 - Means 'to disturb continuously.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 國家設官分職 - Refers to the traditional Chinese system of appointing officials and assigning duties.
  • 前朝仕宦之强爭 - Refers to the political climate of the previous dynasty, where officials competed fiercely for power.
  • 魚肉鄉愚 - A metaphorical expression that reflects the oppression of the common people by the elite.
  • 寧使官家放火，不許百姓點燈之謠 - A proverb that criticizes the harsh policies of the previous dynasty.
  • 江左某巨室 - Refers to a prominent family in the Jiangnan region, known for their political influence.
  • 宰輔 - Refers to high-ranking officials, such as prime ministers or chancellors.
  • 清要 - Refers to important and prestigious positions.
  • 天下名公巨鄉半出其門下 - Indicates the extensive network of influential families and officials connected to the family.
  • 雷令風行 - A metaphor for strict enforcement of orders, reflecting the family's power and influence.
  • 極一時之盛 - Describes the peak of prosperity and influence during their time.
  • 應綿祖德報君恩 - Reflects the Confucian ideal of filial piety and loyalty to the emperor.
  • 以長享富貴耳 - Expresses the desire for long-lasting wealth and honor.
  • 因疾家居 - Indicates that the scholar stayed at home due to illness.
  • 伺應之強奴悍僕以千計 - Describes the large number of servants and slaves who served the scholar.
  • 分班值日，統於老奴 - Describes the organizational structure of the servants.
  • 放班閒曠之時 - Refers to the periods when the servants were not on duty.
  • 成群結夥 - Describes the behavior of the servants gathering in groups.
  • 佔據於茶坊酒肆 - Indicates the places where the servants gathered.
  • 攪擾不休 - Describes the continuous disturbance caused by the servants.

Proper Nouns

  • 國家 - The state or government.
  • 前朝 - The previous dynasty.
  • 江左 - The Jiangnan region.
  • 宰輔 - High-ranking officials, such as prime ministers or chancellors.
  • 清要 - Important and prestigious positions.
  • 天下名公巨鄉 - Eminent officials and influential families.
  • 雷令風行 - A metaphor for strict enforcement of orders.
  • 極一時之盛 - The peak of prosperity and influence.
  • 應綿祖德報君恩 - The virtue of ancestors and loyalty to the emperor.
  • 以長享富貴耳 - Long-lasting wealth and honor.
  • 某學士 - A specific scholar within the family.
  • 因疾家居 - Staying at home due to illness.
  • 伺應之強奴悍僕 - Strong and fierce servants and slaves.
  • 分班值日 - Divided into shifts.
  • 統於老奴 - Managed by an old servant.
  • 放班閒曠之時 - Off-duty and idle times.
  • 成群結夥 - Gathering in groups.
  • 佔據於茶坊酒肆 - Occupying tea houses and taverns.
  • 攪擾不休 - Continuous disturbance.

[206]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有何姓者 - Formal introduction of a person by their surname, meaning 'There was a man surnamed He.'
  • 携其妻女 - Means 'with his wife and daughter,' indicating the family members involved.
  • 开设酒接为业 - Means 'started a business of selling wine,' where '酒接' is a term for a tavern or wine shop.
  • 其悍仆日来饮馔 - Means 'his fierce servant came daily to drink and eat,' where '饮馔' refers to drinking and eating.
  • 逋欠者多 - Means 'many owed him,' where '逋欠' refers to debts or arrears.
  • 一纵性逞兴 - Means 'once he let his temper run wild,' where '纵性' means to let one's nature or temper run free.
  • 号呼达旦 - Means 'shouting and calling until dawn,' where '号呼' refers to loud shouting.
  • 何厌苦之 - Means 'He was very tired of it,' where '厌苦' means to be tired of or disgusted with something.
  • 以酒尽为辞 - Means 'using the excuse that the wine was finished,' where '为辞' means to use as an excuse.
  • 诸仆大怒 - Means 'the other servants were very angry,' where '诸' means 'various' or 'many.'
  • 群搜其室 - Means 'they all searched his house,' where '群' means 'all' or 'together.'
  • 得酒数甕 - Means 'they found several jars of wine,' where '甕' is a large jar or container.
  • 叱问曰 - Means 'they shouted and asked,' where '叱问' means to shout and ask.
  • 此非酒耶 - Means 'Is this not wine?' where '耶' is a particle used to form a question.
  • 奈何欺我 - Means 'Why are you deceiving me?' where '奈何' means 'why' or 'how.'
  • 何方强辩 - Means 'He tried to argue strongly,' where '强辩' means to argue strongly or forcefully.
  • 一醉仆举甕以击何首 - Means 'One drunk servant picked up a jar and hit He on the head,' where '举' means 'to pick up' and '击' means 'to hit.'
  • 立碎之 - Means 'and immediately broke it,' where '立碎' means 'immediately broke.'
  • 漫骂而归 - Means 'and cursed and went back,' where '漫骂' means to curse or scold.
  • 谋诸老奴 - Means 'He consulted with the old servant,' where '谋诸' means 'to consult with.'
  • 告主人以房佃何某不给赁值 - Means 'He reported to the landlord that tenant He did not pay the rent,' where '房佃' means 'tenant' and '赁值' means 'rent.'
  • 使仆索取 - Means 'He sent the servants to collect,' where '使' means 'to send' and '索取' means 'to collect.'
  • 伊触首于墙 - Means 'He hit his head against the wall,' where '触首' means 'to hit the head.'
  • 思饰伤以抵赖 - Means 'He thought to fake an injury to avoid responsibility,' where '饰伤' means 'to fake an injury' and '抵赖' means 'to avoid responsibility.'
  • 不意力猛自戕 - Means 'Unintentionally, he injured himself severely,' where '不意' means 'unintentionally' and '自戕' means 'to injure oneself.'
  • 请杂治之 - Means 'He requested a mixed punishment,' where '杂治' means 'mixed punishment.'
  • 主人从其言 - Means 'The landlord followed his advice,' where '从其言' means 'to follow his words.'
  • 使鸣诸县尹 - Means 'He sent a complaint to the county magistrate,' where '鸣诸' means 'to send a complaint' and '县尹' means 'county magistrate.'
  • 甫闻何妻之报 - Means 'Just as he heard about He's wife's report,' where '甫闻' means 'just heard' and '报' means 'report.'
  • 又得宦词 - Means 'He also received a report from the official,' where '宦词' means 'official report.'
  • 草草验讯 - Means 'He hastily examined and questioned,' where '草草' means 'hastily' and '验讯' means 'to examine and question.'
  • 竟以自戕定谳 - Means 'He ultimately decided it was a suicide,' where '自戕' means 'suicide' and '定谳' means 'to decide a verdict.'
  • 反欲追租完案 - Means 'Instead, he wanted to collect the rent to close the case,' where '追租' means 'to collect rent' and '完案' means 'to close the case.'
  • 何妻茹痛携女 - Means 'He's wife endured pain and took her daughter,' where '茹痛' means 'to endure pain' and '携女' means 'to take her daughter.'
  • 遁于省垣 - Means 'She fled to the provincial capital,' where '遁' means 'to flee' and '省垣' means 'provincial capital.'
  • 含冤莫诉 - Means 'She harbored her grievance and could not complain,' where '含冤' means 'to harbor a grievance' and '莫诉' means 'cannot complain.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 酒接 - A term for a tavern or wine shop, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 悍仆 - A fierce or strong servant, often used to describe someone who is difficult to control.
  • 县尹 - The title of a county magistrate, a local government official in ancient China.
  • 自戕 - A term for suicide, often used in classical texts to describe someone who injures themselves severely.
  • 杂治 - A mixed punishment, which could include various forms of penalties such as fines, beatings, or imprisonment.
  • 省垣 - The provincial capital, a term used to describe the administrative center of a province.

Proper Nouns

  • 何 - The surname of the main character.
  • 县尹 - The title of a county magistrate.
  • 省垣 - The provincial capital.

[207]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 女渐长成，姿容艳丽 - Describes a girl's growth and beauty; 'gradually grew up, with beautiful appearance.'
  • 远近莫不知其美者 - Means 'far and near, no one does not know her beauty'; 'her beauty is widely known.'
  • 恒与母言及父仇，则恸不欲生 - Whenever she spoke with her mother about her father's grudge, she would be so grief-stricken as to not want to live; 'expressing deep sorrow over a personal loss.'
  • 自名曰“仇”，志不忘也 - She named herself 'Chou,' determined not to forget; 'where 'Chou' is likely a pun on 'enemy' or 'grudge,' reflecting her resolve.'
  • 及笄之年 - Refers to the age of fifteen, when a girl traditionally came of age in ancient China.
  • 媒媪造门，问字者踵相接 - Matchmakers came to the door, and those asking for her hand in marriage followed one another; 'indicating a high demand for marriage proposals.'
  • 无论小家巨族，为富为贫，为妻为妾，只须能击强宗，戮悍仆，与父复仇者，以身事之，毋需纳聘 - Regardless of whether they were from humble or noble families, rich or poor, as wife or concubine, only those who could strike down powerful clans, kill fierce servants, and avenge her father would be worthy of her service, without the need for a dowry; 'showing her specific criteria for choosing a husband.'
  • 闻其言者皆咋舌 - Those who heard her words were all speechless; 'indicating surprise or shock at her conditions.'
  • 孝女也，吾当成其志 - She is a filial daughter; I will fulfill her wish; 'expressing the general's intention to help.'
  • 女请得其仇首以祭，偕母投将军，愿长为侍婢以报 - The girl requested to receive the head of her enemy for sacrifice, and together with her mother, she went to the general, willing to serve as a long-term servant in return; 'showing her gratitude and commitment.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 分镇大将军 - A high-ranking military official, often with significant power and influence.
  • 宗室 - A member of the imperial family, indicating a person of high status and potential influence.
  • 缇骑 - Imperial guards, specifically those who carried red belts, often involved in carrying out the emperor's orders.
  • 籍没 - Confiscation of property, a common punishment for crimes against the state.
  • 谪戍 - Exile to the frontier, a form of punishment where individuals were sent to remote areas to serve as soldiers.
  • 大辟 - Capital punishment, the death penalty.

Proper Nouns

  • 仇 - The girl's self-chosen name, likely a pun on 'enemy' or 'grudge.'
  • 分镇大将军 - A title for a high-ranking military official.
  • 宗室 - A term for members of the imperial family.
  • 缇骑 - A term for imperial guards.
  • 籍没 - A legal term for the confiscation of property.
  • 谪戍 - A legal term for exile to the frontier.
  • 大辟 - A legal term for capital punishment.

[208]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 義仆 - Righteous servant or loyal retainer; emphasizes moral integrity and loyalty.
  • 江左望族 - Prominent family in the eastern part of China, specifically in the Jiangnan region; '江左' means 'east of the river,' and '望族' means 'prominent family.'
  • 太守 - Title for a high-ranking local official, equivalent to a governor or prefect.
  • 贿赂公行 - Bribery openly practiced; indicates widespread corruption.
  • 纳资至数十万之富 - Accumulated wealth to the tune of hundreds of thousands; emphasizes the extent of the official's wealth.
  • 孳孽搜求 - Continuously seeking more; suggests an insatiable desire for wealth.
  • 司阍之仆高禄者 - Servant named Gao Lu who serves as a gatekeeper; '司阍' means 'gatekeeper,' and '高禄' means 'high salary' or 'well-paid.'
  • 悖入者必悖出 - What comes in by wrong means will go out by wrong means; implies that ill-gotten gains will eventually be lost.
  • 膝下仅有一公子 - Only one son; emphasizes the official's single heir.
  • 志气纵佚 - Reckless and dissolute; describes a lack of discipline and moral integrity.
  • 少君骄甚 - The young master is very arrogant; highlights the son's arrogance.
  • 裁之以礼 - To discipline with propriety; indicates that the servant attempts to correct the son's behavior using proper etiquette.
  • 淫朋昵友 - Debauched friends and close associates; emphasizes the son's bad company.
  • 匪徒为伍 - To associate with criminals; indicates that the son has turned to a life of crime.
  • 登城席地之局 - Gambling sessions on the city walls; suggests that the son engages in gambling in public places.
  • 囊空爨绝 - Empty purse and no firewood; indicates extreme poverty.
  • 仰屋而嗟 - Look up at the roof and sigh; expresses despair and helplessness.
  • 少夫人 - Young lady or young wife; refers to the official's wife.
  • 江南宦室之女 - Daughter of an official family in Jiangnan; emphasizes her noble background.
  • 美而贤 - Beautiful and virtuous; describes the wife's qualities.
  • 顺为正之义 - The principle of being compliant and correct; indicates that the wife adheres to traditional Confucian values of obedience and propriety.
  • 从无反目事 - Never has there been any conflict; emphasizes the harmonious relationship between the couple.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 江左 - Eastern part of China, particularly the Jiangnan region, which was a center of culture and politics during the Southern Dynasties period.
  • 太守 - Title for a high-ranking local official, similar to a modern governor or prefect.
  • 贿赂公行 - Widespread corruption in the political system of the time, where bribery was openly practiced.
  • 纳资至数十万之富 - Significant wealth that could be accumulated through corrupt practices.
  • 孳孽搜求 - Greed and insatiable desire for wealth that was common among corrupt officials.
  • 司阍之仆高禄者 - Well-paid servant who serves as a gatekeeper, highlighting the social hierarchy and the roles of different individuals in the household.
  • 悖入者必悖出 - Traditional Chinese proverb that emphasizes the moral and karmic consequences of unethical actions.
  • 膝下仅有一公子 - Importance of having a son in traditional Chinese society, where the continuation of the family line was highly valued.
  • 志气纵佚 - Lack of discipline and moral integrity, which was considered a serious flaw in traditional Confucian values.
  • 少君骄甚 - Negative consequences of indulgence and lack of discipline in the upbringing of the son.
  • 淫朋昵友 - Influence of bad company, which was seen as detrimental to one's character and behavior.
  • 匪徒为伍 - Son has turned to a life of crime, reflecting the moral decay that can result from a lack of proper guidance.
  • 登城席地之局 - Son engages in gambling in public places, which was considered a vice in traditional Chinese society.
  • 囊空爨绝 - Extreme poverty, reflecting the dire consequences of the son's reckless behavior.
  • 仰屋而嗟 - Despair and helplessness, emphasizing the son's dire situation.
  • 少夫人 - Official's wife, described as beautiful and virtuous.
  • 江南宦室之女 - Noble background of the wife, reflecting the importance of social status in traditional Chinese society.
  • 美而贤 - Wife's qualities, emphasizing her beauty and virtue.
  • 顺为正之义 - Traditional Confucian values of obedience and propriety, which were highly valued in the relationship between husband and wife.

Proper Nouns

  • 義仆 - Righteous servant
  • 江左望族 - Prominent family in Jiangnan
  • 太守 - High-ranking local official
  • 司阍之仆高禄者 - Servant named Gao Lu who serves as a gatekeeper

[209]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 牛衣对泣 - Literary expression meaning 'weeping in a cowskin cloak,' symbolizing extreme poverty and hardship.
  • 无可如何 - Means 'helpless' or 'powerless to do anything,' indicating a state of despair or hopelessness.
  • 匪徒索逋登门 - '匪徒' refers to outlaws or bandits, and '索逋' means to demand payment of a debt. '登门' means to come to one's door, implying an unexpected visit.
  • 尔家有如许大财，犹诈贫耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Do you have such great wealth and still pretend to be poor?' It is used to accuse someone of hypocrisy.
  • 呆哉 - Exclamation expressing disapproval or contempt, equivalent to 'How foolish!' or 'How stupid!'
  • 尤物 - Refers to something or someone exceptionally beautiful or valuable, often with a connotation of being too precious to be wasted.
  • 诓而货之 - Means 'to deceive and sell it,' referring to the act of tricking someone into selling something valuable.
  • 裘马翩翩 - Phrase describing a wealthy and elegant lifestyle, with '裘' referring to fur coats and '马' to horses. '翩翩' means graceful or stylish.
  • 宿逋顿清 - Means 'to clear all debts at once,' indicating the resolution of financial troubles.
  • 奚济于事 - Rhetorical question meaning 'What good does it do?' or 'How will it help?'
  • 同为饿殍 - Means 'to starve to death together,' referring to a shared fate of poverty and hunger.
  • 苏扬妓室 - Refers to brothels in Suzhou and Yangzhou, two famous cities in Jiangsu Province known for their entertainment industries.
  • 重价相求 - Means 'to offer a high price,' indicating that the brothels would pay a lot for the woman.

  • 大令义之，从其言，传少君与匪至，重惩匪徒，与少君金而教之迁善 - 义之 means found it right, 从其言 means followed his advice, 传少君与匪至 means had the young master and the bandits brought, 重惩匪徒 means severely punished the bandits, 与少君金而教之迁善 means gave the young master money and taught him to change for the better.
  • 禄始送少夫人入都，依其兄弟以终 - 禄 is a name, 始送少夫人入都 means then took the young lady to the capital, 依其兄弟以终 means to stay with her brothers until the end.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 嗟乎 - Interjection expressing sorrow or regret, similar to 'alas' or 'oh.'
  • 孝也而在女，义也而在仆 - Parallel construction emphasizing the inversion of moral expectations, where filial piety is expected from a woman and righteousness from a servant.
  • 俨然人上者 - Refers to those who appear to be above others in status or position.
  • 不孝不义 - Means 'unfilial and unrighteous,' describing a state of moral failure.
  • 古今之变局 - Refers to the changing circumstances of the past and present.
  • 运会之迁移 - Means 'the shifting of fate or destiny.'
  • 明季缙绅为闯贼拷掠者，自取之也 - Statement about the Ming dynasty officials being tortured by rebels, suggesting that their fate was self-inflicted due to their actions or inactions.
  • 身长一十二尺 - Describes a person's height, which is an exaggeration for emphasis.
  • 首大十围，腰圆两抱 - Metaphorical descriptions of a person's large head and thick waist.
  • 拳如巨钵，腿若堂柱 - Metaphors comparing the person's hands and legs to large bowls and pillars, emphasizing their size and strength.
  • 驱牍十余头 - Means 'driving ten oxen,' indicating the person's ability to handle multiple tasks simultaneously.
  • 以三犁并耨 - Refers to using three plows at once, showing the person's efficiency and strength.
  • 往来甚驶 - Means 'moving very quickly,' describing the person's agility.
  • 山雨欲来 - A metaphor for impending danger or trouble.
  • 趋避树下以观 - Means 'seeking shelter under a tree to observe,' indicating the narrator's cautious approach.
  • 担觔饼八枚，牛脯一甕 - Describes the food carried by the younger man, including eight pounds of cake and a jar of beef.
  • 体使不快 - Means 'body discomfort,' suggesting that the person feels unwell without regular physical activity.
  • 两餐需面二百五十六两，牛肉及白酒各三百二十两 - Describes the person's daily food consumption, which is extremely high.
  • 无他能也 - Means 'no other abilities,' emphasizing the person's simplicity and lack of special skills.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 问其力，则曰 - A common pattern in Classical Chinese where someone is asked about their strength or ability, and they respond with a story.
  • 我不自知 - A humble expression meaning 'I do not know myself' or 'I cannot assess my own strength.'
  • 曾忆壮时皇帝东巡过此 - '曾忆' means 'once recalled' or 'once remembered'; '壮时' refers to a time of vigor or youth; '皇帝东巡过此' means 'the emperor passed through here on his eastern tour.'
  • 山下有人熊冲驾而来 - '人熊' refers to a mythical creature, possibly a giant or a man-bear; '冲驾而来' means 'rushed towards the carriage.'
  • 身与我等 - Means 'its body was the same size as mine.'
  • 侍卫之官挡之者，被摘心裂腹而啖 - '侍卫之官' means 'the guards'; '挡之者' means 'those who tried to stop it'; '被摘心裂腹而啖' is a vivid description of the guards being torn apart and eaten.
  • 驾在危急，我不觉直前擒之 - '驾在危急' means 'the carriage was in danger'; '我不觉直前擒之' means 'I unconsciously rushed forward to capture it.'
  • 倒摔于地，而熊首碎竟毙 - '倒摔于地' means 'threw it down to the ground'; '而熊首碎竟毙' means 'its head was crushed and it died.'
  • 皇帝甚喜，欲予我一官 - '皇帝甚喜' means 'the emperor was very pleased'; '欲予我一官' means 'he wanted to give me an official position.'
  • 我对日 - '我对日' means 'I said to him' (the emperor).
  • 小人野田愚民，文不识诗书，武不知战阵，何敢滥膺爵禄以误朝廷 - '小人野田愚民' means 'a lowly rustic fool'; '文不识诗书' means 'I do not know poetry or books'; '武不知战阵' means 'I do not know warfare'; '何敢滥膺爵禄以误朝廷' means 'how dare I accept a title and salary that would mislead the court.'
  • 皇帝益喜，给我一纸书 - '皇帝益喜' means 'the emperor was even more pleased'; '给我一纸书' means 'he gave me a document.'
  • 知之者称之曰诏谕 - '知之者' means 'those who know it'; '称之曰诏谕' means 'call it an imperial decree.'
  • 地方官免我地粮，故至今无催科之扰 - '地方官' means 'local officials'; '免我地粮' means 'exempted me from land taxes'; '故至今无催科之扰' means 'so I have no trouble with tax collectors to this day.'
  • 乃出其诏书与客观 - '乃出其诏书' means 'he then showed the decree'; '与客观' means 'to the observer.'
  • 信不诬也 - Means 'it is true and not false.'
  • 遥指其村落曰 - '遥指其村落' means 'pointed to his village from afar'; '曰' means 'said.'
  • 此皆我子孙之宅 - Means 'these are all the homes of my descendants.'
  • 昔我以一手托梁，一手植柱，身自创者 - '昔我以一手托梁，一手植柱' means 'in the past, I held up a beam with one hand and planted a pillar with the other'; '身自创者' means 'I built it myself.'
  • 虽俱土室，亦甚高大 - '虽俱土室' means 'although they are all earthen houses'; '亦甚高大' means 'they are also very large.'
  • 客计其寿，时已一百二十五岁，力则万夫莫敌矣 - '客计其寿' means 'the guest calculated his age'; '时已一百二十五岁' means 'he was already 125 years old'; '力则万夫莫敌矣' means 'his strength was unmatched by thousands of men.'
  • 归而告人，或疑其妄 - '归而告人' means 'returned and told others'; '或疑其妄' means 'some doubted it was false.'
  • 然客诚朴人也，言当不谬 - '然客诚朴人也' means 'but the guest is a sincere and simple person'; '言当不谬' means 'his words should not be mistaken.'
  • 芗厈日，防风之骨可专车，长狄之身横九亩，巨人自古有之，不足奇 - '芗厈日' is a reference to a historical figure or event; '防风之骨可专车' means 'the bones of Fangfeng can fill a cart'; '长狄之身横九亩' means 'the body of Changdi spans nine acres'; '巨人自古有之，不足奇' means 'giants have existed since ancient times, and it is not surprising.'
  • 所可异者，富寿而多裔，立功而不居，惟圣人之邦，斯有此贤隐士耳 - '所可异者' means 'what is remarkable'; '富寿而多裔' means 'wealthy, long-lived, and with many descendants'; '立功而不居' means 'achieved great deeds but did not seek recognition'; '惟圣人之邦，斯有此贤隐士耳' means 'only in a land of sages can such a virtuous recluse be found.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 皇帝东巡过此 - Refers to the emperor's eastern tour, a significant event in Chinese history.
  • 山下有人熊冲驾而来 - '人熊' is a mythical creature, possibly a giant or a man-bear, often used in Chinese folklore.
  • 侍卫之官挡之者，被摘心裂腹而啖 - Describes a dramatic and violent scene, typical of heroic tales.
  • 皇帝甚喜，欲予我一官 - Reflects the imperial system where loyalty and bravery could lead to rewards.
  • 小人野田愚民，文不识诗书，武不知战阵，何敢滥膺爵禄以误朝廷 - A humble and modest statement, typical of the era's social hierarchy.
  • 皇帝益喜，给我一纸书 - The emperor's response shows his appreciation and reward for the hero's actions.
  • 知之者称之曰诏谕 - '诏谕' is a formal imperial decree, emphasizing the importance of the document.
  • 地方官免我地粮，故至今无催科之扰 - Local officials' exemption of taxes is a sign of the hero's status and the emperor's favor.
  • 乃出其诏书与客观 - The act of showing the decree to an observer adds a layer of verification.
  • 信不诬也 - Confirms the truthfulness of the story.
  • 遥指其村落曰 - The hero's connection to his village and descendants highlights his legacy.
  • 此皆我子孙之宅 - Emphasizes the hero's role in building and maintaining his community.
  • 昔我以一手托梁，一手植柱，身自创者 - A vivid description of the hero's physical strength and craftsmanship.
  • 虽俱土室，亦甚高大 - Describes the modest yet impressive nature of the village.
  • 客计其寿，时已一百二十五岁，力则万夫莫敌矣 - Highlights the hero's longevity and strength.
  • 归而告人，或疑其妄 - Reflects the skepticism and curiosity of others.
  • 然客诚朴人也，言当不谬 - The guest's credibility is established by his straightforward and honest nature.
  • 芗厈日，防风之骨可专车，长狄之身横九亩，巨人自古有之，不足奇 - References to mythical figures and events, emphasizing the hero's uniqueness.
  • 所可异者，富寿而多裔，立功而不居，惟圣人之邦，斯有此贤隐士耳 - Praises the hero's virtues and the rarity of such a person in a sage's land.

Idiomatic Expressions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 至中元节 - Formal expression indicating the arrival of the Zhongyuan Festival, a traditional Chinese holiday.
  • 解馆而归 - Literally means 'to leave the academy and return home,' indicating the end of a period of study.

  • 江南之望族也 - Refers to Wang as a prominent family from the Jiangnan region.
  • 屈为小官 - Means 'was forced to take a low-ranking position,' indicating a sense of委屈 (feeling wronged or humiliated).
  • 未免负才肆志 - Means 'inevitably expressed his talents and ambitions,' suggesting a sense of frustration or dissatisfaction.
  • 是日太尊按临触其怒 - Means 'on that day, the Grand Magistrate's inspection provoked his anger,' where '按临' means 'to inspect.'
  • 欲黜之 - Means 'wanted to demote him.'
  • 幸大令为之营求稍解 - Means 'fortunately, the County Magistrate intervened and somewhat alleviated the situation,' where '营求' means 'to intervene' or 'to mediate.'
  • 尉送太尊登舟 - Means 'the County Magistrate sent the Grand Magistrate off on his boat.'
  • 求见谢过 - Means 'asked for an audience to apologize.'
  • 太尊呼跪船头 - Means 'the Grand Magistrate called him to kneel at the bow of the boat.'
  • 严加训饬 - Means 'severely reprimanded him,' where '训饬' means 'to reprimand' or 'to scold.'
  • 久而释之 - Means 'after a long time, he was released.'
  • 尉愧且忿 - Means 'the County Magistrate felt ashamed and angry.'
  • 甫登舆 - Means 'as soon as he got into the carriage.'
  • 适俞生葛衣布履 - Means 'just then, Yu, wearing a coarse robe and straw sandals, came.'
  • 为人所拥挤 - Means 'was pushed by the crowd.'
  • 以盖柄触肩舆之玻璃而碎之 - Means 'accidentally hit the glass of the shoulder carriage with the handle of his umbrella and broke it.'
  • 尉大怒 - Means 'the County Magistrate became very angry.'
  • 叱役牵来 - Means 'ordered his servants to bring him.'
  • 俞不禁战栗下跪 - Means 'Yu could not help but tremble and kneel down.'
  • 问之不能答 - Means 'when asked, he could not answer.'
  • 喝行杖 - Means 'ordered the beating to begin.'
  • 左右捉俞，去裤按之地 - Means 'his attendants seized Yu, pulled off his pants, and forced him to lie down.'
  • 杖甫一下，俞忽醒 - Means 'as soon as the stick was about to strike, Yu suddenly woke up.'
  • 大呼曰：“打不得，打不得！” - Means 'shouted, 'Do not beat me, do not beat me!''
  • 诘之，对曰：“吾生员也。” - Means 'when questioned, he replied, 'I am a scholar.''
  • 告以姓名，尉惶恐 - Means 'when told his name, the County Magistrate became alarmed.'
  • 下舆手扶之，再三谢罪 - Means 'got off the carriage, held him by the hand, and apologized repeatedly.'
  • 俞摇首结裤而起 - Means 'Yu shook his head, put on his pants, and stood up.'
  • 与阁下无干，此其中盖有神明也 - Means 'it has nothing to do with you; there must be some divine intervention here.'
  • 仍执其盖而去 - Means 'he took his umbrella and left.'
  • 尉回衙，亦自悔卤莽 - Means 'the County Magistrate returned to his office and also regretted his hasty action.'
  • 是对观者甚众，典史擅责生员之说，传作奇闻 - Means 'the incident was witnessed by many people, and the story of the Chief Clerk beating a scholar spread as a strange tale.'
  • 同学诸生知之，佥来问询 - Means 'his fellow scholars learned about it and came to ask about it.'
  • 俞曰：“是诚有之，然系神明所使，吾不见过。” - Means 'Yu said, 'It is true, but it was done by divine intervention; I did not do anything wrong.''

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夢虛而事實 - The dream was unreal, but the facts were real; contrast between imagination and reality.
  • 一人受辱，辱及同侪 - One person was humiliated, and the disgrace extended to their peers; highlights collective responsibility.
  • 吾等能不与较耶 - How could we not respond? Rhetorical question expressing determination.
  • 拥俞入县署，鸣鼓声冤 - They escorted Yu into the county office and beat the drum to appeal for justice; formal act of protest.
  • 大令亟召学师来议有以解释之法 - The magistrate urgently summoned the schoolmaster to discuss a way to resolve the matter; formal investigation.
  • 兔死狐悲，物伤其类 - When the rabbit dies, the fox mourns; things grieve for those of their kind; metaphor for shared suffering.
  • 众怒不可犯也 - The anger of the crowd cannot be provoked; emphasizes the danger of collective anger.
  • 猛虎项下铃，系者自能解之 - A bell on a tiger's neck; the one who tied it can remove it; metaphor for self-responsibility.
  • 尉虑诸生攻击之，潜避县署 - The military officer, fearing attack from the students, secretly avoided the county office; indicates fear of confrontation.
  • 长跪师前日 - He knelt long before the teacher; sign of deep respect or apology.
  • 我之误挞俞先生，追悔莫及 - I mistakenly beat Mr. Yu; I deeply regret it; expresses remorse.
  • 俞先生器局宏大，初未与我较 - Mr. Yu has a broad and magnanimous character; he did not initially hold a grudge against me; highlights Yu's forgiveness.
  • 众怒不可犯也，然猛虎项下铃，系者自能解之 - The anger of the crowd cannot be provoked, but the bell on the tiger's neck can only be removed by the one who tied it; metaphor for self-responsibility.
  • 大令亦参赞之 - The magistrate also advised; indicates support for the resolution.
  • 师出问，俞摇首曰 - The teacher asked, and Yu shook his head, saying; indicates Yu's refusal to engage.
  • 门人不愿与较，只求有以慰众心耳 - My students do not wish to confront them; they only seek to appease the crowd; expresses Yu's desire for peace.
  • 罚之何如 - What about punishing them? Suggestion for resolution.
  • 众曰：“愿闻罚之之法。” - They said, 'We wish to hear the method of punishment'; indicates the crowd's demand for justice.
  • 当众令其拜俞生以谢过 - Have them kneel before Yu in public to apologize; formal act of atonement.
  • 众目视俞，俞犹摇首曰 - The crowd looked at Yu, and Yu still shook his head, saying; indicates Yu's continued refusal.
  • 不愿庭辱长官 - I do not wish to humiliate the officials in court; expresses Yu's respect for authority.
  • 众谓师曰 - The crowd said to the teacher; indicates their persistence.
  • 既俞生之盛德，应令其摒去从人，执香步行至俞府跪拜，吾等释之 - Given Mr. Yu's great virtue, we should have them dismiss their followers, carry incense, and walk to Yu's residence to kneel and apologize; we will then release them; detailed plan for reconciliation.
  • 师曰：“诺。” - The teacher said, 'Agreed'; indicates agreement with the plan.
  • 俞同众回，具衣冠洒扫门庭，烹茗以俟 - Yu returned with the crowd, dressed in formal attire, cleaned the courtyard, and prepared tea to wait; indicates Yu's readiness to receive the apology.
  • 未几，尉公服持香而来 - Soon, the military officer arrived in formal attire, carrying incense; indicates the officer's compliance.
  • 俞远出慰劳，辞香献茗，极其诚敬，即回拜之 - Yu came out to comfort him, declined the incense, and offered tea with great sincerity and respect; indicates Yu's graciousness.
  • 众皆无可如何而散 - The crowd had no choice but to disperse; indicates the resolution of the conflict.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 居停辞之曰 - 居停 refers to the host or landlord, and 辞之 means to refuse or decline. This phrase indicates the host's refusal.
  • 昔吾以先生为英才也 - 昔 means formerly, 吾 means I, 以...为 means to consider...as, and 英才 means talented person. This phrase expresses the host's initial high regard for the guest.
  • 故敢屈诸西席以为门庐光 - 故 means therefore, 敢 means dare, 屈 means to humble oneself, 诸 is a particle, 西席 means teacher, and 以为门庐光 means to bring glory to the household. This phrase explains the host's initial reason for inviting the guest.
  • 今已受挞于市，为众讪笑 - 今 means now, 已 means already, 受挞 means to be beaten, 于市 means in the market, and 为众讪笑 means to be ridiculed by the crowd. This phrase describes the current situation where the guest has been publicly humiliated.
  • 则吾子弟人皆目之为罪人之弟子也 - 则 means then, 吾子弟人 means my family members, 皆 means all, 目之 means to regard, 为 means as, and 罪人之弟子 means disciples of criminals. This phrase explains the negative perception of the guest's family.
  • 何辱如之 - 何 means how, 辱 means shame, and 如之 means like this. This phrase emphasizes the extreme shame.
  • 敢辞 - 敢 means dare, and 辞 means to refuse. This phrase reiterates the host's refusal.
  • 俞无以自明 - 俞 is the name of the guest, 无以 means unable to, and 自明 means to defend oneself. This phrase indicates the guest's inability to explain his situation.
  • 抱惭而归 - 抱惭 means with shame, and 而归 means and return. This phrase describes the guest's return home in shame.
  • 然尉甚德之 - 然 means however, 尉 is the name of the official, 甚 means very, and 德之 means respected him. This phrase indicates the official's respect for the guest.
  • 中秋登门贺节 - 中秋 means Mid-Autumn Festival, 登门 means to visit, and 贺节 means to congratulate on the festival. This phrase describes the official's visit to congratulate the guest.
  • 俞见之，色甚不怿 - 俞 is the name of the guest, 见之 means saw him, 色 means expression, 甚 means very, and 不怿 means unhappy. This phrase describes the guest's unhappy expression.
  • 尉叩其情 - 尉 is the name of the official, 叩 means to inquire, and 其情 means his situation. This phrase indicates the official's inquiry into the guest's situation.
  • 俞告以失馆故 - 俞 is the name of the guest, 告 means to tell, 以 means because, and 失馆故 means the reason for losing his position as a tutor. This phrase explains the guest's reason for losing his position.
  • 为我而有屈贤才，我罪大矣 - 为我 means for me, 而有屈贤才 means to委屈 a talented person, and 我罪大矣 means my sin is great. This phrase expresses the official's regret.
  • 然馆谷不足虑，敬为先生推毂 - 然 means however, 馆谷 means lodging and food, 不足虑 means not to worry about, 敬为 means respectfully for, and 推毂 means to recommend. This phrase indicates the official's offer to recommend the guest.
  • 岁有增修，俞阅十余年归家，积千金，称小康云 - 岁有增修 means yearly increases, 俞 is the name of the guest, 阅 means spend, 十余 means more than ten, 年 means years, 归家 means return home, 积千金 means accumulate a thousand gold pieces, and 称小康云 means is considered moderately wealthy. This phrase describes the guest's financial success.
  • 谦，福之基也 - 谦 means humility, and 福之基也 means the foundation of happiness. This phrase introduces the commentary.
  • 故《谦》卦六爻皆吉 - 故 means therefore, 《谦》卦 means the Hexagram of Humility, 六爻 means six lines, and 皆吉 means all are auspicious. This phrase cites the *I Ching*.
  • 俞生自受神诫之后，何其谦也 - 俞生 is the name of the guest, 自受神诫之后 means after receiving divine instruction, and 何其谦也 means how humble he is. This phrase describes the guest's humility.
  • 随责之而随富之，冥冥中假王尉之手 - 随责之 means as he faced responsibilities, 而随富之 means and became wealthy, 冥冥中 means in the unseen, 假 means through, and 王尉之手 means the hand of the official. This phrase suggests that the guest's success was divinely guided.
  • 由是观之，为人处世，可不谦乎哉 - 由是观之 means from this perspective, 为人处世 means in dealing with people and affairs, and 可不谦乎哉 means how can one not be humble? This phrase concludes the commentary.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 次日 - Literally 'the next day,' a common temporal marker in Classical Chinese.
  • 催廉访速究以报 - A bureaucratic expression meaning 'urge the censor to investigate quickly and report back.'
  • 制军濡笔待奏 - Literally 'the general governor wet his brush waiting to write a memorial,' a metaphor for preparing to write an official report.
  • 许大怖 - A colloquial expression meaning 'Xu Da was terrified.'
  • 叩首无算 - Literally 'knocked his head countless times,' a hyperbolic way of saying he prostrated himself repeatedly.
  • 自称死罪 - Literally 'claimed his own death as a crime,' meaning he admitted guilt.
  • 若稍纵之，吾去后老獠必变 - A conditional phrase meaning 'if you let him off lightly, after I leave, the old rascal will surely change,' with '老獠' being a derogatory term for the father.
  • 骤富 - Literally 'suddenly wealthy,' meaning becoming rich quickly.
  • 奋勉读书 - Literally 'strive to study diligently,' meaning to work hard at studying.
  • 入泮 - A term referring to entering the school, specifically the local Confucian school.
  • 登贤书 - Literally 'list among the talented,' meaning to be listed as a talented scholar.
  • 刚愎故 - Literally 'stubbornly obstinate,' describing the general governor's character.
  • 被仇家陷害 - Literally 'was framed by enemies,' meaning to be falsely accused by opponents.
  • 奉旨拏问 - Literally 'imprisoned by imperial decree,' meaning to be arrested by order of the emperor.
  • 因乃父之门生故旧为之营救 - Literally 'due to his father's former students and acquaintances who helped to rescue him,' meaning that the father's connections helped to save him.
  • 铨得县令出仕 - Literally 'was appointed as a county magistrate and began his official career,' meaning to be promoted to a government position.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 廉访 - Refers to a censor, a high-ranking official responsible for supervising and reporting on local affairs.
  • 制军 - Refers to a general governor, a high-ranking military and administrative official.
  • 书院 - Refers to a traditional Chinese institution of higher learning, often associated with Confucian education.
  • 家财 - Refers to family wealth or property.
  • 制军力 - Refers to the authority or power of the general governor.
  • 诵读之资 - Refers to the resources or means for reading and studying.
  • 刚愎 - Describes a person who is stubborn and obstinate, often used to criticize someone's character.
  • 仇家 - Refers to enemies or opponents.
  • 门生故旧 - Refers to former students and acquaintances, often used to describe a network of connections.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence describes the general governor's actions in response to the censor's investigation. The sequence of events must be clear: the censor is urged to investigate quickly, and the general governor prepares to write a report.
  • This sequence describes Xu Da's actions and words. The sentence must be read as a continuous narrative: Xu Da is terrified, rushes to the academy to see his son-in-law, prostrates himself repeatedly, and admits his guilt.
  • This conditional phrase must be read as a warning: if the son-in-law lets Xu Da off lightly, the old man will surely change his behavior after the general governor leaves.
  • This clause describes the rescue efforts. The sentence must be read as a causal relationship: because of the connections with the father's former students and acquaintances, the son-in-law was saved.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 老妪主政 - Governed by an old woman; reflects the traditional Chinese practice of matriarchal authority.
  • 规矩井然 - Strictly adhering to rules and regulations; emphasizes the strict adherence to social norms in a noble household.
  • 秀外慧中 - Beautiful on the outside and wise within; describes the exceptional qualities of a young girl.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 老妪主政 - Reflects the traditional Chinese practice of matriarchal authority in some families.
  • 家人婢仆数十人 - Indicates a large household with numerous family members and servants, reflecting the social structure of a wealthy or influential family.
  • 教师十余人 - The presence of multiple teachers suggests a focus on education and cultural refinement within the household.
  • 诸秀女各有所业 - Highlights the diverse roles and talents of young women in the household, reflecting the importance placed on education and personal development.
  • 规矩井然，宛尔大家风范 - Emphasizes the strict adherence to social norms and the high standards of behavior expected in a noble household.
  • 所雇徐孺人，教诸女刺绣耳 - The hiring of a tutor for embroidery reflects the importance of traditional crafts and the value placed on artistic skills.
  • 见其带女来，年逋十三四 - The mention of a young girl around thirteen or fourteen years old suggests a focus on the upbringing and education of young women.
  • 秀外慧中，超越诸女之上 - Describes the exceptional qualities of the young girl, emphasizing both her physical beauty and intellectual prowess.
  • 因俾母女与己共食，易女以时服，嘱同诸女入塾读书 - Reflects the care and attention given to the young girl, ensuring she is integrated into the household and provided with the best possible education.
  • 暇时调琴作画，以及吹弹歌舞之事，与女伴逞能竞敏，亦精绝无伦 - Describes the diverse activities and talents of the young girl, highlighting her artistic and social skills.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '其家以老妪主政，家人婢仆数十人，咸尊之曰‘老太太’' introduces the setting and the central figure, the elderly woman who governs the household.
  • The sentence '教师十余人，诸秀女各有所业，称妪为母、为祖母、为老老者，莫不娇容丽质，举止安娴' provides more details about the household, including the number of teachers and the roles of the young women.
  • The sentence '其家法，则三尺之童，非奉呼唤，不敢入中门，规矩井然，宛尔大家风范' describes the strict rules and the noble demeanor of the household.
  • The sentence '所雇徐孺人，教诸女刺绣耳' introduces a specific detail about the household's activities and the role of the hired tutor.
  • The sentence '见其带女来，年逋十三四，秀外慧中，超越诸女之上，妪甚怜之' introduces a new character, the young girl, and describes her qualities and the elderly woman's affection for her.
  • The sentence '因俾母女与己共食，易女以时服，嘱同诸女入塾读书，暇时调琴作画，以及吹弹歌舞之事，与女伴逞能竞敏，亦精绝无伦' describes the integration of the young girl into the household and her activities and talents.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 當徐孺人初至時 - Introduces the narrative in a classical style.
  • 問妪門第 - Asked the old woman about her family background.
  • 以丈夫子皆外出服官 - Classical term for serving in government.

  • 金陵风俗皆如是 - '金陵' is the classical name for Nanjing.
  • 不能违也 - '违' means to violate or go against.
  • 至中堂 - '中堂' is a central hall or main hall in a house.
  • 见公子髯而伟 - '髯' means beard, and '伟' means tall or imposing.
  • 仆从甚众 - Classical term for servants or attendants.
  • 有指点肥瘦者 - Refers to people commenting on body types or physical appearance.
  • 有品评妍媸者 - '妍媸' is a classical term for beauty and ugliness.
  • 见孺人偕女来 - '偕' means together or with.
  • 公子傲不为礼 - '不为礼' means to not perform the proper rites or show respect.
  • 坐而观之 - Classical expression meaning to sit and watch or observe.
  • 一仆指之曰 - Narrative technique used to introduce a character's comment.
  • 此女骨秀神清，超前绝后，诚尤物也 - '骨秀神清' is a classical expression for refined and elegant beauty. '尤物' is a term for an exceptionally beautiful woman, often with a connotation of being captivating or even dangerous.
  • 不意粪壤中竟产灵芝 - '粪壤' is a metaphor for lowly or unrefined origins, and '灵芝' is a rare and precious mushroom, often used as a metaphor for something rare or exceptional.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 徐孺人 - '孺人' is a classical honorific title for a woman, often used in official or literary contexts.
  • 门第 - Key concept in classical Chinese society, especially in the context of marriage and social standing.
  • 及笄 - A coming-of-age ceremony for girls, typically at age 15, indicating that a girl is now eligible for marriage.
  • 择婿 - The act of choosing a husband for a daughter, often involving a matchmaker or family negotiations.
  • 相女 - A term used in the context of marriage proposals, where a suitor would inspect the bride.
  • 中堂 - The central hall of a house, often used for formal events or meetings.
  • 金陵 - The classical name for Nanjing, a major city in southern China.
  • 尤物 - A term used to describe an exceptionally beautiful woman, often with a connotation of being captivating or even dangerous.
  • 粪壤 - A metaphor for low or unrefined origins.
  • 灵芝 - In classical Chinese culture, '灵芝' is a symbol of longevity, purity, and divine power, often associated with immortality and virtue.

Proper Nouns

  • 金陵 - The classical name for Nanjing, a major city in southern China.
  • 灵芝 - A rare and precious mushroom, symbolizing rarity and exceptional beauty in classical Chinese culture.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 府县严刑鞫实 - Rigorous interrogation and investigation conducted by local officials.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 方幼樗云 - A phrase indicating that the following content is a commentary or opinion attributed to a person named '方幼樗'.
  • 俨然史公《管晏列传》笔法 - '俨然' means 'as if' or 'exactly like,' and '史公《管晏列传》笔法' refers to the writing style of Sima Qian in his biography of Guan Zhong and Yan Ying.
  • 一则吴生详叙于前，一则赵生之妻胪陈于首 - '一则' means 'one part' or 'one aspect,' and '胪陈' means 'to list in detail' or 'to enumerate.'
  • 绝似天际神龙，总不令首尾并见 - '绝似' means 'exactly like' or 'very much like,' '天际神龙' refers to a mythical dragon in the sky, and '总不令首尾并见' means 'always keeping the beginning and end hidden.'
  • 文心变幻笔墨间，大得烟云之助 - '文心' means 'literary heart' or 'literary spirit,' '变幻' means 'changing' or 'transforming,' '笔墨间' means 'in the brush and ink,' and '大得烟云之助' means 'greatly aided by the mist and clouds.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentences are elliptical and require the reader to infer the full meaning, such as '数千金订某贡生代倩' (a large sum of money being used to hire a recommended scholar to take the examination on behalf of someone else) and '不意某临场骤丁外艰' (the recommended scholar unexpectedly encountering the death of his father).
  • The sentences are elliptical and require the reader to infer the full meaning, such as '吾等先曾询得足下系赴试者' (the speaker and others had previously inquired and found out that the listener was preparing to take the examination) and '是以引入妓馆，以缚足下之身，而盗取监照，俾某顶名与试' (the speaker and others had brought the listener to a brothel, bound him, and stolen his examination certificate to allow the recommended scholar to take the examination under his name).
  • The sentences are elliptical and require the reader to infer the full meaning, such as '其命也夫' (the listener realizes that the outcome was fated or predestined) and '科场大弊，性命相关' (the corruption in the examination system is of life-and-death importance).
  • The sentences are elliptical and require the reader to infer the full meaning, such as '何先不谋诸我' (why the listener did not consult or discuss the matter with the speaker first) and '欲我冒作孝廉，不可，不可' (the listener is refusing to pretend to be a filial and upright official).
  • The sentences are elliptical and require the reader to infer the full meaning, such as '我寒士也，无力作举人' (the listener is a poor scholar and does not have the means to become a successful candidate) and '万金之资，不为少矣' (the amount of money offered is not small).
  • The sentences are elliptical and require the reader to infer the full meaning, such as '科第之荣，不为贱矣' (the honor of passing the examination is not insignificant) and '我之才力，何足以进取' (the listener believes his abilities are not sufficient to achieve success).
  • The sentences are elliptical and require the reader to infer the full meaning, such as '与其悖入悖出，宁藏其拙' (the listener prefers to keep his limitations hidden rather than risk gaining something unjustly and then losing it unjustly) and '得以尽仰事俯育之心足矣' (the listener is satisfied with being able to support his parents and nurture his children).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 沈終日閒暇，惟潛窥太守延客，退與幕中人議論儀注而已 - This sentence describes Shen's daily activities, but the phrase '惟潛窥太守延客' is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean that Shen is secretly observing the governor entertaining guests, or it could imply that he is only involved in such activities.
  • 同事者入相學，覺其腹內空空 - The phrase '覺其腹內空空' is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean that the colleagues realize that Shen's knowledge is shallow, or it could imply that they feel empty or unfulfilled in his presence.
  • 然以丞相私人，皆欲得其歡心 - The phrase '然以丞相私人' is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean that Shen is a private person of the chancellor, or it could imply that he is a close associate of the chancellor.
  • 兄如有事參商，弟輩當力圖報效 - The phrase '兄如有事參商' is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean that Shen has something he needs help with, or it could imply that he is seeking assistance from his colleagues.
  • 勿以外人目之，則幸甚 - The phrase '勿以外人目之' is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean that Shen does not want to be seen as an outsider, or it could imply that he wants to be accepted as part of the group.
  • 沈知眾心之向己也，故居停所嘱之事，皆倩人主稿 - The phrase '故居停所嘱之事，皆倩人主稿' is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean that Shen delegates the matters he is staying to handle to others, or it could imply that he relies on others to draft the main documents.
  • 如是者半載，心切不安 - The phrase '如是者半載' is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean that Shen continues this practice for half a year, or it could imply that he feels restless and uneasy during this period.
  • 自計儀注已習，可以歸矣 - The phrase '自計儀注已習' is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean that Shen believes he has mastered the ceremonial protocols, or it could imply that he feels ready to return.
  • 遂告太守曰，昨有都中人來，奉中堂諭，喚予入都，未審其何事也 - The phrase '未審其何事也' is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean that Shen does not know the purpose of the summons, or it could imply that he is uncertain about what is expected of him.
  • 然不敢逗留，請辭 - The phrase '然不敢逗留' is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean that Shen feels obliged to leave immediately, or it could imply that he is eager to depart.

  • 前缘 - A concept from Buddhist philosophy, referring to a predestined or karmic connection between individuals.
  • 太尊与太夫人 - Respectful terms for the deceased father and mother, emphasizing their high social status.
  • 及笄 - A traditional Chinese term indicating that a girl has reached the age of marriage (15 for women).

Proper Nouns

  • 灵隐寺 - Lingyin Temple, a famous Buddhist temple in Hangzhou.
  • 大唐 - The Tang Dynasty, a period of great cultural and artistic achievement in Chinese history.
  • 先觉 - A name or title, possibly referring to a person with foresight or enlightenment.
  • 冯御史 - An imperial censor named Feng, indicating a high-ranking official.
  • 太尊 - A respectful term for the deceased father.
  • 太夫人 - A respectful term for the deceased mother.
  • 大公子 - The eldest son, indicating a high social status.

[262]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 先覺知父母已故 - 先覺 can mean 'a pre-eminent sage' or 'an enlightened one,' and '知父母已故' means 'knew that his parents had passed away.'
  • 大恸几绝 - '大恸' means 'great grief,' and '几绝' means 'almost died' or 'nearly lost consciousness.'
  • 随家人回见，乃兄劝之易服毕姻 - '随家人回见' means 'followed his family back home,' '乃兄' means 'his elder brother,' '劝之' means 'advised him,' '易服' means 'to change clothes,' and '毕姻' means 'to complete the wedding.'
  • 我与汝了此一段因缘，数乃前定 - '了' means 'to conclude' or 'to finish,' and '一段因缘' means 'a segment of karmic connection.' '数乃前定' means 'the fate was predestined.'
  • 不通人道，留此无益 - '不通人道' means 'cannot interact with humans,' and '留此无益' means 'staying here is of no benefit.'
  • 我从此行矣，去留由汝 - '从此行矣' means 'I will leave from here,' and '去留由汝' means 'whether to leave or stay is up to you.'
  • 我知之已久，亦愿归依佛法 - '我知之已久' means 'I have known this for a long time,' and '亦愿归依佛法' means 'I also wish to follow Buddhism.'
  • 坐逾两载，功益精进 - '坐逾两载' means 'sat for more than two years,' and '功益精进' means 'progressed increasingly in his practice.'
  • 忽闻车轰雷吼之声，山谷震动，见大蟒探首欲相食 - '忽闻车轰雷吼之声' means 'suddenly heard the sound of a car like thunder,' '山谷震动' means 'the mountains and valleys shook,' and '见大蟒探首欲相食' means 'saw a large python sticking its head out to eat him.'
  • 先觉疑神静摄，听之而已 - '疑神静摄' means 'doubtfully but calmly focused,' and '听之而已' means 'just listened to it.'
  • 自忖此地缘满，故妖魔起 - '自忖此地缘满' means 'thought that the karma here was complete,' and '故妖魔起' means 'therefore, demons arose.'
  • 遂入都经历各寺，未遇高人，终日静定而已 - '遂入都经历各寺' means 'then went to the capital and visited various temples,' '未遇高人' means 'did not meet any great masters,' and '终日静定而已' means 'spent the whole day in a state of calmness.'
  • 眉目间光华灿烂，迥别凡流 - '眉目间光华灿烂' means 'his eyebrows and eyes were radiant with light,' and '迥别凡流' means 'completely different from ordinary people.'
  • 诸王与公卿大臣见而异之，共保举入万寿寺方丈 - '诸王与公卿大臣见而异之' means 'kings, princes, and high officials were amazed by him,' and '共保举入万寿寺方丈' means 'jointly recommended him to become the abbot of Wanshou Temple.'
  • 坐年余，获布施万余金 - '坐年余' means 'served as abbot for over a year,' and '获布施万余金' means 'received donations worth tens of thousands of gold pieces.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 先觉 - Refers to an enlightened being or sage.
  • 罗汉果 - Refers to the attainment of Arhatship, a stage in Buddhist practice.
  • 玉大士像 - Refers to a jade statue of the Great Master, likely a Bodhisattva or a revered figure in Buddhism.
  • 燕山之西域寺 - Refers to the Western Temple of Yan Mountains, a specific location.
  • 万寿寺 - Refers to Wanshou Temple, a significant Buddhist temple.
  • 五台 - Refers to Mount Wutai, one of the Four Sacred Mountains of Chinese Buddhism.
  • 嘉峪关 - Refers to the Jiayu Pass, a historical pass in the Great Wall of China.
  • 西域 - Refers to the Western Regions, areas west of China, often associated with Buddhist pilgrimage sites.

[263]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 游终南山 - To tour Mount Zhongnan; a common expression referring to traveling or visiting a sacred or scenic mountain.
  • 遍拜诸寺 - Visited all the temples; indicates a pilgrimage or religious visitation.
  • 佥在山之阳 - All of them were on the southern (yang) side of the mountain; in Classical Chinese, yang refers to the sunny side of a mountain.
  • 其山阴相隔黄流 - The northern (yin) side of the mountain was separated by the Yellow River; yin refers to the shaded side of a mountain.
  • 望之树木阴森，无敢入者 - Looking at it, the trees appeared ominous and gloomy, and no one dared to enter; yin sen means gloomy or sinister.
  • 即有好事之人，去亦不反 - Even if there were those with a liking for adventure, they would not return; hao shi zhi ren refers to people who are curious or adventurous.
  • 相传为栖真之所 - It is said to be a place where true sages or immortals dwell; qi zhen refers to the dwelling place of sages or immortals.
  • 先觉欣然愿往 - The one who had awakened (to the Dao) was eager to go; xian jue refers to someone who has attained spiritual insight or enlightenment.
  • 携带干粮，渡河而入 - Carrying dry food, he crossed the river and entered; gan liang is a classical term for dry provisions or food for travel.
  • 日则攀葛结藤，夜则调息入定 - During the day, he climbed vines and grasped lianas; at night, he regulated his breath and entered meditation; diao xi ru ding refers to a meditative state achieved through breath control.
  • 始出树林，则现奇峰叠嶂，千态万状 - Upon emerging from the forest, he saw strange peaks and layered mountains, each with a thousand forms and ten thousand shapes; qian tai wan zhuang is a classical expression for innumerable forms and shapes.
  • 或茏葱以高，或崭崱而削，或崇岺以幽，或崎岖而险 - Some were lush and towering, some sharp and sheer, some lofty and secluded, some winding and perilous; long cong = lush; zhan zhi = sharp, steep; chong ci = lofty hills; qi qu = winding, rugged.
  • 径路盘曲，似有人行者 - The path was winding, as if someone had walked it; pan qu means winding or coiled.
  • 复数日出山坳，则势更嵣莽，莫穷莫尽 - After several more days, emerging from the mountain pass, the landscape became even more vast and boundless, with no end in sight; sang mang is a classical term for vast, wild, or untamed.
  • 秀色可餐 - A classical idiom meaning the beauty is so striking that one could eat it; used to describe breathtaking scenery.
  • 遥望山腰，时见石龛隐隐，有人趺坐 - Looking afar at the mountain’s waist, one could sometimes see faint stone niches, with someone sitting in meditation; fu zuo refers to the cross-legged sitting posture of meditation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 终南山 - A famous mountain in Shaanxi Province, traditionally considered a sacred place of Daoist and Buddhist pilgrimage and meditation.
  • 先觉 - A term used in Daoist and Buddhist texts to refer to someone who has attained spiritual awakening or insight.
  • 调息入定 - A Daoist and Buddhist practice of breath control and entering a meditative state.
  • 栖真之所 - A place where sages or immortals dwell, often used in Daoist or Buddhist contexts.
  • 石龛 - Stone niches or alcoves, often used in temples or caves for meditation or housing statues of deities.
  • 趺坐 - The cross-legged sitting posture used in meditation, common in both Buddhism and Daoism.
  • 黄流 - A classical term for the Yellow River, which is a major river in China and often symbolizes natural boundaries or obstacles in classical texts.

Proper Nouns

  • 终南山 - Mount Zhongnan, a well-known sacred mountain in Chinese tradition.
  • 黄流 - The Yellow River, a major river in northern China.

[264]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 第四面峭壁，无路可通 - Literal meaning: 'the fourth side is a sheer cliff, with no path to pass through.' Describes an impassable natural obstacle.
  • 先觉呼之不应，膜拜之亦不答 - Describes actions of calling out and prostrating oneself to show respect or seek attention.
  • 迎面一古衣冠道人来 - Literary device using '古衣冠' (ancient attire) to describe sages or immortals.
  • 予凡夫耳，焉得仙？仙人肯使汝见耶？ - Humble and self-deprecating statement often used by sages or immortals to downplay their status.
  • 即于石上穿之，得水与饮 - Vivid description of obtaining water from the stone.
  • 清凉渗骨 - Common idiom describing something extremely cool or refreshing.
  • 连给二匙，收藏欲去 - Polite way of indicating departure.
  • 弟子尚未足也 - Humble way of expressing dissatisfaction.
  • 不但汝渴已已，即不食，亦无碍矣 - Hyperbolic statement emphasizing the other person's condition.
  • 何必多饮 - Rhetorical question used to advise moderation.
  • 犹执裾不释 - Vivid description of the act of clinging to someone.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 先觉 - Name or title given to the main character, possibly a scholar or practitioner of spiritual practice.
  • 秦皇时为役夫 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Qin Shi Huang, the first emperor of China.
  • 楚汉争雄 - Power struggle between Xiang Yu and Liu Bang after the fall of the Qin dynasty.
  • 乌江之败 - Battle of Gaixia where Xiang Yu was defeated by Liu Bang.
  • 佩匙 - Pendant or amulet, often worn by scholars or practitioners for protection or good fortune.

Proper Nouns

  • 先觉 - Name or title of the main character.
  • 秦皇 - Emperor Qin Shi Huang, the first emperor of China.
  • 楚汉 - Refers to the states of Chu and Han during the late Qin dynasty.
  • 乌江 - Location of the Battle of Gaixia.
  • 徐姓 - Surname of the old man, indicating his lineage or background.

[265]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 哀求超度 - To plead for salvation or liberation, often used in Buddhist contexts.
  • 道不同，不相为谋 - Classical saying meaning 'if the ways are different, they cannot plan together,' indicating incompatible paths.
  • 绝裾而去 - Poetic description of someone leaving abruptly, as if tearing off one's robe.
  • 不食不饥 - Describes a state of spiritual or physical lightness, feeling neither hungry nor full.
  • 山深不知处耳 - Means 'the mountains are deep, so I don't know where he is,' expressing uncertainty about someone's whereabouts.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 华疯子 - Nickname given to the character, indicating his eccentric behavior.
  • 幼名多官 - Means 'his childhood name was Duoguan,' providing background on the character's early life.
  • 读书不就 - Means 'he did not succeed in his studies,' describing his academic failure.
  • 不事别业 - Means 'he did not engage in other professions,' indicating his lack of focus on practical work.
  • 终日游荡 - Means 'wandering all day,' describing his aimless lifestyle.
  • 见鸣钲开道者过，随之入衙 - Vivid description of someone following a procession into an official building, with '鸣钲' referring to the sound of a bell or drum.
  • 观其升堂理事，詈人挞人皆不敢违拗 - Detailed account of observing someone in power, with '升堂理事' meaning 'to ascend the hall and handle affairs,' and '詈人挞人' meaning 'scolding and beating people.'
  • 心甚艳之 - Means 'he greatly admired it,' expressing his desire for power.
  • 归家演习 - Means 'returning home to practice,' describing his imitation of authority figures.
  • 亲友俱以疯子目之 - Means 'his friends and relatives all called him a madman,' indicating his eccentric behavior.
  • 逢人则问：官从何来？ - Means 'he would ask people: 'Where does the official come from?'' showing his obsession with authority.
  • 有诳之者曰：“官易事耳，但须北京去见皇帝，求之必得。” - Means 'someone lied to him, saying: 'Being an official is easy; you just need to go to Beijing to see the emperor, and you will surely get it.'' This is a deceptive statement meant to mislead him.
  • 窃其父金数两，往北逃逸 - Means 'he stole several taels of gold from his father and fled north,' describing his criminal act.
  • 至浦口，一无所有矣 - Means 'upon reaching Pu Port, he had nothing left,' indicating his financial ruin.
  • 遇官舫之赴楚者觅纤手，昧然应募 - Means 'he encountered a government boat going to Chu and, in a dazed state, volunteered as a boatman,' describing his desperate situation.
  • 昧然 - Means 'in a dazed or confused state,' indicating his mental state.
  • 众知其疯，皆玩戏之 - Means 'everyone knew he was crazy and played tricks on him,' showing how others treated him.
  • 惟官舫中一叟屡自顾之，周其衣食 - Means 'only an old man on the government boat repeatedly looked after him, providing him with food and clothing,' highlighting the kindness of one person.

Proper Nouns

  • 华三祝 - Character's name, reflecting his eccentric and unpredictable behavior.
  • 北京 - Capital city of China, often associated with authority and power.
  • 皇帝 - Emperor, the supreme ruler in ancient China.
  • 浦口 - A location where the character reaches after his journey.
  • 楚 - A historical region in China, often associated with ancient states.

[266]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一日遇飓风 - One day encountered a typhoon, describing a specific event.
  • 停泊闲地 - Anchored in a quiet place, indicating a location where the ship was temporarily moored.
  • 华在小庙檐下坐卧 - Describes the protagonist, Hu, sitting and lying under the eaves of a small temple, a vivid image of his condition.
  • 忽叟来邀之入庙 - Suddenly an old man came and invited him into the temple, introducing a mysterious figure.
  • 密语之曰 - Whispered to him saying, indicating a private conversation.
  • 舫内之官是吾子张某 - The official on the boat is my son Zhang, revealing a familial relationship.
  • 其貌与汝无异 - His appearance is no different from yours, highlighting the similarity between the two individuals.
  • 即至亲亦难辨认 - Even close relatives would find it hard to recognize him, emphasizing the difficulty in distinguishing them.
  • 以吏员铨得楚县尉 - He was appointed as the county magistrate of Chu through the official selection process, describing the official's position.
  • 至楚赴任 - He went to Chu to take up his post, indicating his journey to the new position.
  • 不意昨得暴疾卒矣 - Unfortunately, he died suddenly yesterday, expressing the unexpected nature of the death.
  • 我与眷属贫不能回家 - My family and I are poor and cannot return home, explaining their dire situation.
  • 依我计议 - Follow my plan, indicating the old man's proposal.
  • 可将汝衣与我子互易 - You can exchange clothes with my son, suggesting a practical solution.
  • 汝承我子职 - You will take over my son's position, outlining the new role for Hu.
  • 官中所得割半均分 - Split the income from the office equally, detailing the financial arrangement.
  • 则两全其事矣 - And both of us will benefit, summarizing the outcome of the plan.
  • 华如其所愿 - Hu followed his wishes, indicating compliance with the old man's plan.
  • 疯痰顿愈 - His madness and phlegm suddenly improved, describing a sudden change in Hu's condition.
  • 是夜，潜相易换 - That night, they secretly exchanged clothes, detailing the execution of the plan.
  • 明日，舟子唤疯子 - The next day, the boatman called for the madman, indicating a routine action.
  • 则见其倒毙于庙矣 - And found him lying dead in the temple, revealing the tragic outcome.
  • 叟为买棺厝之 - The old man bought a coffin and buried him, showing his care for the deceased.
  • 遂赴任 - And then took up his post, indicating Hu's new role.
  • 分衙署为两宅 - He divided the government office into two residences, describing the physical changes.
  • 与眷属各居 - And lived separately with his family, indicating the living arrangements.
  • 嫂事张妻 - His sister-in-law took care of Zhang's wife, showing the new family dynamics.
  • 叟为经理出入 - The old man managed the affairs in and out, indicating his role in managing the household.
  • 荏苒三年 - Three years passed gradually, describing the passage of time.
  • 各分得千金 - Each of them received a thousand gold coins, detailing the financial outcome.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 飓风 - Refers to a typhoon, a common natural disaster in China, particularly in coastal areas.
  • 小庙 - Refers to a small temple, a common place of worship and refuge in traditional Chinese society.
  • 吏员铨得楚县尉 - The official selection process (铨) was a system used in ancient China to appoint officials. The position of 县尉 (county magistrate) was a local administrative role.
  • 楚 - Refers to the state of Chu, an ancient Chinese kingdom, often used as a geographical reference.
  • 疯痰 - Refers to mental illness and phlegm, a common medical term in classical Chinese literature.
  • 疯子 - Refers to a madman, a person suffering from mental illness.
  • 舟子 - Refers to a boatman, a person who operates a boat.
  • 衙署 - Refers to a government office or administrative building.
  • 嫂 - Refers to a sister-in-law, a term used to describe a woman who is the wife of one's brother.
  • 荏苒 - A literary term meaning 'gradually' or 'slowly,' often used to describe the passage of time.
  • 千金 - Refers to a large sum of money, equivalent to a thousand gold coins, a significant amount in ancient times.

Proper Nouns

  • 华 - The protagonist of the story.
  • 叟 - The old man who proposes the plan.
  • 张 - The name of the official and Hu's new identity.
  • 楚 - The state of Chu, where the official was appointed.
  • 县尉 - The position of county magistrate.
  • 嫂 - The sister-in-law who takes care of Zhang's wife.
  • 舟子 - The boatman who calls for the madman.
  • 衙署 - The government office or administrative building.
  • 千金 - A large sum of money, equivalent to a thousand gold coins.

[267]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 忽聞 - Suddenly heard or suddenly learned of, indicating a sudden piece of news.
  • 莅任 - To arrive in office or to take up a new post, commonly used for officials assuming a new position.
  • 探知為龚姓 - Learned that he was surnamed Gong, a way of discovering someone's identity.
  • 系华氏姑母之子 - Is the son of the aunt of the Hu family, indicating a familial relationship.
  • 以科甲由部曹出守是邦者 - A person who passed the imperial exams and was promoted from a central government official to become the magistrate of this region. This is a complex phrase indicating a career path through the imperial examination system.
  • 难与相见 - Difficult to meet, suggesting that the person is too high in status or too respected to be approached casually.
  • 然在其属下，不敢不谒 - But as his subordinate, one dared not not pay respects, a double negative emphasizing obligation.
  • 以张姓职名入见 - Presented himself with the surname Zhang and his official title, indicating how he introduced himself.
  • 太守睹其状貌，核其履历 - The magistrate observed his appearance and examined his resume, a formal process of assessment.
  • 心甚疑之 - He was very suspicious of him, suggesting doubt or uncertainty.
  • 入见太夫人 - Enter to see the magistrate's wife, indicating an audience with the magistrate’s wife.
  • 言其故 - He explained the reason, used to describe the explanation given.
  • 太夫人泣曰 - The magistrate's wife wept and said, a dramatic expression of emotion.
  • 尔舅父仅有一子，不知何往 - Your uncle had only one son, whose whereabouts are unknown, indicating a missing relative.
  • 吾亦欲观其貌，纵非真者，藉以解渴想耳 - I also want to see his appearance; even if it is not the real one, it will at least relieve my longing. A poetic expression of deep emotional yearning.
  • 太守唯唯 - The magistrate nodded in agreement, a term indicating polite assent.
  • 立命传见 - Immediately ordered him to be summoned, indicating swift action.
  • 华俯首攒眉 - Hua bowed his head and furrowed his brow, a gesture of deep anxiety or apprehension.
  • 置身无地 - Had no place to stand, a metaphor for being in a state of confusion or helplessness.
  • 惟有叩首而已 - Could only kneel and bow, indicating submission or helplessness.
  • 详加体察 - Carefully observed and examined, used to describe a thorough inspection.
  • 是吾侄也 - This is my nephew, a direct statement of identity.
  • 奈何勿认？ - Why not recognize him? A rhetorical question expressing confusion or frustration.
  • 华益惶恐曰 - Hua became even more terrified and said, indicating a heightened emotional state.
  • 野鸟何敢冒凤 - How dare a wild bird pretend to be a phoenix? A metaphor comparing himself to a lowly creature daring to impersonate nobility.
  • 貌相似者，自古有之 - Similar appearances have existed since ancient times, a common saying to justify the situation.
  • 孔子阳贷，圣狂不类耳 - Confucius and Yangdai were not alike; the sage and the madman are not the same. A reference to the historical figure Yangdai, a supposed madman who resembled Confucius, used to illustrate that even if one looks like a great figure, they may not be one.
  • 即告退 - Then he withdrew, indicating the end of the encounter.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太守 - The magistrate or governor of a county, a key official in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 部曹 - A central government official, often in the Ministry of Personnel or similar.
  • 出守是邦 - To be appointed as the magistrate of this region, a common career path for officials who passed the imperial exams.
  • 华氏 - The Hu family, a reference to a noble or influential family.
  • 龚姓 - The surname Gong, indicating the new magistrate.
  • 科甲 - Refers to the imperial examination system, particularly the *keju* (科举), which was the main route to officialdom in imperial China.
  • 太夫人 - The term used for the wife of a high-ranking official, often with honorific connotations.
  • 孔子阳贷 - A reference to the historical anecdote involving Confucius and Yangdai (阳贷), a man who supposedly looked like Confucius but was not a sage. This is a well-known story used to illustrate the danger of mistaking appearance for identity.
  • 圣狂不类耳 - The sage and the madman are not the same, a contrast between true virtue and mere resemblance.

Proper Nouns

  • 龚姓 - The surname Gong, referring to the new magistrate.
  • 华氏 - The Hu family, a noble or influential family.
  • 太夫人 - The magistrate’s wife, a title rather than a name.
  • 孔子 - Confucius, the great sage of ancient China.
  • 阳贷 - A historical figure or legend, often associated with the story of someone who resembled Confucius but was not a sage.

[268]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太夫人未能挽留 - Indicates an attempt to stop someone from leaving.
  • 目送之 - A poetic way of saying she watched him go.
  • 幼名曰 - Refers to a term of endearment or familiarity.
  • 多官儿安在耶 - A rhetorical question expressing concern or surprise.
  • 不觉回顾 - Describes an involuntary action.
  • 急使仆婢群拥之入 - Indicates a sudden and forceful action.
  • 作色视之 - Shows anger or disapproval.
  • 汝是吾侄华三祝也 - Identifies the person.
  • 何得背父母瞒亲戚 - A strong reproach.
  • 好官自为耶 - A sarcastic or dismissive remark.
  • 窘极 - Describes a state of distress.
  • 叩首曰 - A formal gesture of respect or submission.
  • 非也 - A simple denial.
  • 吾自呼多官儿 - A reference to a previous action.
  • 何劳汝回首 - A rhetorical question expressing frustration.
  • 再欲强辩 - Indicates a warning.
  • 命汝表兄以官法治汝矣 - A threat.
  • 直陈始末 - Indicates honesty.
  • 骇曰 - Shows shock or disbelief.
  • 此事性命相关 - Emphasizes the seriousness.
  • 弟奈何为之 - A question expressing disappointment or anger.
  • 其速回乃任 - A command.
  • 与张叟交割 - Indicates a transfer of responsibility.
  • 以公事参革 - A formal action.
  • 嘱张叟代报病故 - A deception.
  • 潜回家乡 - Indicates a covert action.
  • 以真姓名别纳一官可也 - A suggestion for a new start.
  • 得以父子团聚 - Indicates a positive outcome.
  • 保其首领以殁 - A euphemism for dying peacefully.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太夫人 - A respectful term for a mother-in-law or a mother of a prominent person.
  • 幼名 - A term of endearment used in families.

Idiomatic Expressions

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 先王 - Refers to a former king or emperor.
  • 王 - Refers to the current king or emperor.
  • 殿 - Refers to a palace or grand hall.
  • 匠 - Refers to a skilled craftsman or builder.

  • 华毂 - Refers to an ornate carriage.

  • 定契银 - Refers to a deposit or down payment for a contract.
  • 拆换 - Refers to the act of dismantling and replacing structures.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '其人曰：“先王成此殿，费十万金，汝愿以若干售之，不妨明言。”' is a direct quote from a person, introducing the negotiation.
  • The sentence '匠曰：“材料己旧，大而无当，将必改为小用。不过万金而已。”' is a direct quote from the craftsman, providing his valuation.
  • The sentence '其人故作不愿，往来数次，曰：“王今无奈，姑以售汝，当在某庄立券，汝先往俟之。”' describes the negotiation process and the eventual agreement.
  • The sequence '是日，朱转华毂，王果至庄。其人为前驱，带匠人入庄，王南面坐，匠跽请书券，王颔之，命从官书就，亲笔签押，先索定契银三千两，余俟拆换之日准算。' describes the signing of the contract and the initial payment.
  • The sentence '其人偕匠入城交银，并索费银，亦先给三百，约日而散。' describes the payment of additional fees and the agreement on a future date.
  • The sequence '至日，大匠带人往拆殿廷，门官拒之，匠告以故，门官入白王，王召匠人，仰观王貌，非前日之人矣。知为拐骗，无言而退。' describes the craftsman's attempt to dismantle the palace and the discovery of the fraud.
