  • 世习申韩 - Indicates that the person's family has been practicing the teachings of Shen Buhai and Han Fei, both prominent figures in Legalist philosophy.
  • 由幕而官 - Describes a career path from working as a consultant or advisor (幕僚) to becoming an official.
  • 去官复幕 - Refers to the cycle of leaving official positions and returning to advisory roles.
  • 保阳待聘 - Means waiting in BaoYang (保阳) for employment opportunities.
  • 住旅寓有时矣 - Indicates that the person has been living in temporary accommodations for some time.
  • 迫切钻营 - Describes intense efforts to seek advancement or success.
  • 无微不至 - Means thorough and meticulous, often used to describe someone who goes to great lengths to achieve their goals.
  • 是年冬 - Refers to a specific winter during the year mentioned.
  • 章服华焕 - Describes richly decorated official clothing.
  • 仆从趋跄 - Refers to servants moving in a respectful and orderly manner.
  • 往来之客络绎不绝 - Describes a continuous flow of visitors.
  • 似皆宪司之纪纲也 - Suggests that the visitors seem to be officials or subordinates of the provincial administration.
  • 大名县总司阍 - Refers to the gatekeeper of the magistrate of Daming County.
  • 奉本官命 - Means acting under the orders of the magistrate.
  • 来省置办衣饰为少君完姻 - Indicates the purpose of the visit is to purchase clothing for the magistrate's daughter's wedding.
  • 延访善于刑名、钱谷之士 - Means seeking out experts in legal matters and financial administration.
  • 以易旧友者 - Suggests replacing old friends with new ones.
  • 探得其情 - Means discovering the situation or circumstances.
  • 具衣冠往拜 - Describes dressing formally and going to pay respects.
  • 稍闲 - Means having a bit of free time.
  • 盘桓 - Refers to lingering or spending time together.
  • 竟成莫逆 - Means eventually becoming close friends.
  • 性情慷爽 - Describes a person as generous and straightforward.
  • 目不识丁 - Means being illiterate.
  • 漏已三下 - Refers to the third strike of the night watch, indicating late at night.
  • 突有役来传本官谕帖 - Describes a sudden messenger arriving with a message from the magistrate.
  • 催其人归去 - Means urging the person to return.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 申韩 - Refers to Shen Buhai and Han Fei, key figures in Legalist philosophy.

  • 少君 - A respectful term for a young lady, often used for the daughter of a noble or official.
  • 刑名、钱谷 - Refers to legal matters and financial administration, respectively.
  • 莫逆 - A term for deep friendship or mutual understanding.

Proper Nouns

  • 保阳 - A specific location in China.
  • 大名县 - A specific location in China.
  • 少君 - A respectful term for a young lady, often used for the daughter of a noble or official.
  • 本官 - Refers to the magistrate or official in charge.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 叩倪寝门而告之曰 - Knocked on Mr. Ni's bedroom door and told him; a formal and respectful way of addressing someone.
  • 烦为一读来书 - Please read the incoming letter; a polite request.
  • 有役守候，不能待旦矣 - There is a servant waiting, and we cannot wait until dawn; indicating urgency.
  • 不过因办公乏人 - It is just because there is a lack of personnel for office work; explaining the situation.
  • 谕令迅将什物置妥即去 - Commanded to quickly arrange the items and leave; a direct instruction.
  • 并旧友辞定 - And to finalize the farewell with old friends; indicating a final act before departure.
  • 所访之新友，必须于开篆以前到署等语 - The new friend to be visited must arrive at the office before the start of the term; specifying a deadline.
  • 踟蹰曰 - Hesitated and said; indicating uncertainty or deliberation.
  • 办物不难，访友非易 - Arranging the items is not difficult, but visiting friends is not easy; contrasting two tasks.
  • 意中有信托之人乎 - Do you have someone you trust in mind?; a question about trustworthiness.
  • 自述其历就州县，助其东人升迁者不一 - Described how he had served in various prefectures and counties, helping many officials from the east to advance; a detailed account of past experiences.
  • 吾以先生为记室耳，畴知申韩妙手近在邻居，而由外求人耶 - I thought you were just a secretary, who knew that a master of Shang and Han is right next door and not seeking someone from outside?; a realization and surprise.
  • 即请代某禀复云 - Immediately asked to write a reply on behalf of someone; a request for assistance.
  • 以千金订定倪先生，俟各物办齐即回，不敢逗留也 - To engage Mr. Ni with a thousand gold, to return immediately after all items are arranged, and not to linger; a formal agreement.
  • 倪为书就，交役竟去 - Mr. Ni wrote the letter and handed it over to the servant, who then left; a conclusion to the interaction.

  • 开篆 - Refers to the beginning of an official term or period, often used in administrative contexts.
  • 州县 - Refers to prefectures and counties, administrative divisions in ancient China.
  • 东人 - Refers to people from the east, often used to denote a specific region or group.

  • 申韩妙手 - Refers to someone skilled in the legalist philosophy of Shang Yang and Han Feizi.
  • 邻居 - Refers to a neighbor, indicating proximity and familiarity.
  • 由外求人 - Refers to seeking someone from outside, often implying a lack of internal resources or trust.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 忝在同署 - A humble expression meaning 'to be unworthy to be in the same office' or 'to be in the same office despite one's inadequacy.'
  • 尚何可疑 - Means 'how can there be any doubt' or 'there is no reason to doubt.'
  • 是足下多心矣 - A polite way of saying 'you are being overly suspicious' or 'you are being too cautious.'
  • 即偕赴缎局 - Means 'immediately went together to the silk bureau' or 'went together to the silk bureau right away.'
  • 缘所定之物实千余金 - Means 'the goods ordered were worth over a thousand taels of silver.'
  • 仅付百两 - Means 'only paid a hundred taels.'
  • 余皆挂欠 - Means 'the rest was all on credit' or 'the rest was all outstanding.'
  • 未肯与也 - Means 'was unwilling to give' or 'refused to provide.'
  • 局主已访得大名 - Means 'the bureau head had already learned of your distinguished name.'
  • 确有姻事 - Means 'there is indeed a marriage affair' or 'there is a real marriage matter.'
  • 倪就其幕 - Means 'Ni joined his entourage' or 'Ni became part of his staff.'
  • 被倪至殷勤款接 - Means 'was treated with great hospitality by Ni' or 'was warmly welcomed by Ni.'
  • 尔铺主不我信耶 - Means 'Does your shop owner not trust me?'
  • 请以先生为质 - Means 'please take Mr. as a guarantee' or 'please have Mr. serve as a surety.'
  • 明年来请先生时 - Means 'when you come to invite Mr. next year.'
  • 其价原车带到 - Means 'the price will be brought by carriage' or 'the payment will be delivered by carriage.'
  • 决无贻误 - Means 'there will be no delay' or 'there will be no mistake.'
  • 倪亦言同事之故 - Means 'Ni also explained the reason for being colleagues' or 'Ni also mentioned the reason for being colleagues.'
  • 愿力任之 - Means 'willing to take full responsibility' or 'willing to assume full responsibility.'
  • 局主欣然交倪货物 - Means 'the bureau head gladly handed over the goods to Ni' or 'the bureau head happily gave the goods to Ni.'
  • 给其人满载而去 - Means 'gave the person a full load to take away' or 'provided the person with a full load to leave with.'
  • 无车来接 - Means 'no carriage came to pick up' or 'there was no carriage to receive.'
  • 问司阍并无其人 - Means 'asked the gatekeeper and found no such person' or 'inquired of the gatekeeper and found no one.'
  • 亦无易幕事 - Means 'there was also no change in the entourage' or 'there was also no change in the staff.'
  • 倪归，货其行李以偿铺账 - Means 'Ni returned, sold his belongings to pay off the shop's debt' or 'Ni came back, sold his luggage to settle the shop's account.'
  • 悔恨无及 - Means 'regretted it too late' or 'regretted it beyond remedy.'

  • 铺主 - Refers to the owner of a shop or store.
  • 局主 - Refers to the head or manager of a bureau or office.
  • 司阍 - Refers to a gatekeeper or doorkeeper, responsible for managing access to a building or compound.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence describes the innkeepers' eagerness to accommodate the servants who visited multiple households, but none were satisfied.
  • This sentence explains that at this time, the ginseng business had specialized staff to serve guests, and this information was spread throughout various businesses.
  • This sentence indicates that the two servants went as far as a mile away, and all the innkeepers knew about it, so they were willing to offer a high price.
  • This sentence describes the servant's joy and the innkeepers sending their staff to take ginseng, the customer weighing one ounce to taste it, finding it worth more than three hundred, and exchanging it for seven pieces of silver.
  • This sentence describes the extravagant display inside the public hall, the discovery that the lady needed to take three ounces of ginseng per month, the significant transactions, and the arrival of her family soon.
  • This sentence describes the innkeeper's pleasure, the customer arriving in plain clothes by carriage, and stating that the ginseng was of true value and quality, needed for the lady's preferences.
  • This sentence describes the innkeeper presenting the best ginseng, selecting forty ounces, sending two staff members to carry the ginseng and exchange for payment, and instructing them to deliver the sealed silver after the exchange was completed.
  • This sentence describes the two staff members going together to the public hall, entering the room, and quickly reaching the back hall.

  • 此我乡亲某刺史也 - 'This is a relative of mine, a certain Magistrate (or Governor).' '刺史' is a title for a regional governor or magistrate.
  • 其向我借贷屡矣 - 'He has borrowed from me many times.' A passive construction indicating repeated borrowing.
  • 不可使彼登楼，见如许物，则更扰累不清矣 - 'We cannot let him go upstairs, for if he sees all these things, it will only cause more disturbances and confusion.' This is a complex conditional sentence with a cause-effect structure.
  • 使仆以参及银皆归箱内锁之 - 'He ordered the servant to put the ginseng and silver back into the box and lock it.' This is a directive with a list of items.
  • 闻被来客强拉之行 - 'He heard that the guest was being forcibly taken away.' This is a passive construction indicating that the guest was taken by force.
  • 烦客略坐，守去即来也 - 'Please the guest to sit for a moment, I will return shortly.' A polite request with a promise to return.
  • 乃反扣楼门而去 - 'He then locked the door from the inside and went away.' This is a narrative action with a clear sequence.
  • 旋闻幼仆数人在楼下戏谑 - 'Soon after, the sound of several young servants joking was heard downstairs.' A descriptive phrase indicating a shift in scene.
  • 始而喧哗，继而揪斗 - 'At first, there was noise, then a scuffle.' A sequence of events.
  • 有老苍头来，吆喝不应，鞭挞从之 - 'An old servant came, shouted, but was not answered, and was then beaten.' This is a narrative of conflict and punishment.
  • 幼仆不服，哭声震耳 - 'The young servant was不服 (unconvinced or不服从, not obeying), and his crying could be heard clearly.' '不服' can mean 'not convinced' or 'not obedient,' depending on context.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 洋表时钟 - 'Western watches and clocks' refers to foreign goods introduced to China during the Qing Dynasty, likely through trade with Europe or the West. These items were considered luxury goods and symbols of foreign influence.
  • 刺史 - A title of a regional governor or magistrate in the Chinese administrative system. It was commonly used during the Han, Tang, and later dynasties.
  • 乡亲 - 'Relative' or 'fellow villager,' indicating a personal connection rather than a formal title.
  • 参 - Ginseng, a highly valued medicinal herb in Chinese culture, often used as a gift or in trade.
  • 银封 - A silver seal or wax seal used to secure a box or chest, indicating that the contents are valuable or important.
  • 秤兑 - Weighing and exchanging, likely referring to the process of verifying the weight of silver before use or exchange.
  • 西音 - A term for Western speech or language, indicating that the guest is a foreigner or speaks a foreign language.
  • 虎邱 - Possibly a place name, but the exact location is unclear. It could be a local landmark or a reference to a specific event or festival.
  • 苍头 - A term for an elderly servant or male servant, often used in classical Chinese to denote a senior or trusted servant.
  • 鞭挞 - Beating or flogging, a form of corporal punishment used in feudal or household settings.

Proper Nouns

  • 虎邱 - A proper noun, possibly a place or event. Uncertain exact location, but likely a local landmark.

  • 其人即背负之曰 - Narrative phrase meaning 'The person then carried it on his back and said.'
  • 如是登岸竟去 - Phrase meaning 'He disembarked in the same way and left.'
  • 仆方笑曰 - Phrase meaning 'The servant laughed and said.'
  • 此人何其呆也 - Rhetorical question meaning 'How foolish this person is!'
  • 自弃其米为人负重妆点何为耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Why would he abandon his rice and let someone carry his burden?'
  • 舟子曰 - Phrase meaning 'The boatman said.'
  • 此人一去不顾，恐是骗子 - Statement meaning 'This person left without a care, probably a scammer.'
  • 仆悟，急追，不知所之矣 - Narrative phrase meaning 'The servant realized and rushed to follow, but lost sight of him.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乘舟 - Refers to traveling by boat, a common mode of transportation in ancient China.
  • 访亲 - Refers to visiting relatives, a social practice in ancient China.
  • 河涘 - Refers to the riverside, a common location for daily activities like washing rice.
  • 竹箩 - A type of bamboo basket used for carrying goods.
  • 淘洗 - Refers to washing rice, a common household activity.
  • 头舱 - Refers to the bow of the boat, where luggage is often stored.
  • 贼 - Refers to a thief, indicating a common concern for safety in ancient China.
  • 舟子 - Refers to the boatman, who is responsible for the boat's operation.
  • 骗子 - Refers to a scammer, indicating a common concern for fraud in ancient China.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 赴岁试 - To take the annual examination, referring to the regular imperial examination system.
  • 行李迁起 - Moving the luggage, describing the act of transferring belongings.
  • 尚未议定脚价 - The price for carrying the luggage has not been agreed upon, indicating the negotiation stage.
  • 站坐行李上守之 - Standing or sitting on the luggage to guard it, a colloquial expression indicating vigilance over one's belongings.
  • 忽有衣冠而来者 - Suddenly a person dressed in formal attire appeared, a common way to describe someone of higher social status.
  • 兄台何自来耶 - What brings you here, sir? A polite and formal way to inquire about someone's presence.
  • 误矣 - It was a mistake, a polite way to correct a misunderstanding.
  • 揖谢不安而别 - Bowed and left in embarrassment, indicating a formal departure after a brief interaction.
  • 仰跌倒地 - Fell backward to the ground, a vivid description of a physical reaction.
  • 方知行李已为人抽去 - Realized that the luggage had been taken away, indicating the realization of a theft.
  • 龙有嗜，可豢之 - Dragons have their preferences, and they can be tamed, a metaphorical expression suggesting that even powerful beings have vulnerabilities.
  • 物先腐，虫生之 - Objects decay first, and then pests infest them, a metaphorical expression indicating that problems often arise from internal weaknesses.
  • 诸公皆有隙可乘 - All of you have weaknesses that can be exploited, a statement about the inherent vulnerabilities of people.
  • 入其玄中 - To enter their hidden depths, a metaphorical expression suggesting penetration into the inner workings of something.
  • 回视人间世，了无一事真 - Looking back at the human world, nothing is truly real, a philosophical statement about the nature of reality.
  • 诚哉是言也 - Indeed, that is true, a phrase affirming a statement.
  • 癏阓中百货杂伪 - In the bustling market, goods are mixed with fakes, a description of the prevalence of counterfeit items.
  • 医者充市 - Doctors fill the market, a hyperbolic expression indicating the abundance of medical practitioners.
  • 畴为张长沙、刘河间耶 - Who are the Zhang Changsha and Liu Hejian? A rhetorical question about the authenticity of medical practitioners.
  • 星者盈街 - Astrologers fill the streets, a hyperbolic expression indicating the abundance of astrologers.
  • 畴为李虚中、袁天罡耶 - Who are the Li Xuzhong and Yuan Tiangan? A rhetorical question about the authenticity of astrologers.
  • 当今入幕之宾 - The current people in the inner circle, referring to influential individuals.
  • 名为习申、韩者 - Those who claim to study Shen and Han, referring to followers of the Legalist philosophers Shen Buhai and Han Feizi.
  • 不知申、韩是一是二，是何代人，何曾读不害、非子之书耶 - Do not know whether Shen and Han are one or two, or what dynasty they belonged to, or have ever read the books of Buhei and Feizi? A critical question about the knowledge of these followers.
  • 九流杂技固不足道 - The nine schools of thought and various skills are not worth mentioning, a dismissive statement about certain intellectual pursuits.
  • 科甲遍宇内 - The imperial examination system is widespread throughout the land, a statement about the prevalence of the examination system.
  • 何者为班、马、屈、宋之词华 - Who are the Ban, Ma, Qu, and Song who are known for their literary excellence? A rhetorical question about the authenticity of literary figures.
  • 何者为周、程、张、朱之理学 - Who are the Zhou, Cheng, Zhang, and Zhu who are known for their rationalist philosophy? A rhetorical question about the authenticity of philosophical figures.
  • 叩之茫然者居多 - Most people are at a loss when asked, indicating a lack of genuine knowledge.
  • 不过以八股时文骗功名耳 - They only use the eight-part essays to cheat for official positions, a critical statement about the superficial nature of the examination system.
  • 纷纷一大世界 - A chaotic world, a metaphorical expression describing a disordered society.
  • 人骗己，己骗人 - People deceive each other, a statement about mutual deception.
  • 有时己亦骗己 - Sometimes people deceive themselves, a statement about self-deception.
  • 何怪乎骗子手耶 - Why is it surprising that there are so many deceivers? A rhetorical question about the prevalence of deception.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 岁试 - Refers to the annual imperial examinations, a key part of the Chinese civil service examination system.
  • 衣冠 - Refers to formal attire, often associated with scholars and officials.
  • 兄台 - A respectful term for a male peer or elder.
  • 龙 - Symbolizes power and nobility in Chinese culture.
  • 张长沙、刘河间 - Refers to Zhang Zhongjing (Zhang Changsha) and Liu Jing (Liu Hejian), famous physicians from the Han dynasty.
  • 李虚中、袁天罡 - Refers to Li Xuzhong and Yuan Tiangan, famous astrologers from the Tang dynasty.
  • 申、韩 - Refers to Shen Buhai and Han Feizi, prominent Legalist philosophers.
  • 九流 - Refers to the Nine Schools of Thought, including Confucianism, Daoism, Legalism, and others.

  • 周、程、张、朱 - Refers to Zhou Dunyi, Cheng Hao, Cheng Yi, Zhang Zai, and Zhu Xi, prominent Neo-Confucian philosophers.

  • 其药以龟溺熬紫草为之 - Means 'the medicine was made by boiling turtle urine with purple grass.'
  • 即染鬃缨之法 - Means 'it was the same method used to dye horsehair and ribbons.'

  • 俾终生为红胡子矣 - Means 'he was thus destined to have red hair for life.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 及家，雨至 - A simple statement of sequence, 'Upon arriving home, rain came.'
  • 闻轰击之声甚厉 - Describes the sound of thunder as 'very loud,' with '轟擊' literally meaning 'thunderous impact.'
  • 立唤仆之妻子 - 'Immediately called the servant's wife and children,' with '立唤' meaning 'immediately called.'
  • 告以故 - 'Told them the reason,' with '告以故' meaning 'informed them of the reason.'
  • 皆号泣欲往护之 - 'All cried and wanted to go protect him,' with '號泣' meaning 'cried out' and '欲往護之' meaning 'wanted to go protect him.'
  • 其主不允 - 'The master did not allow it,' with '不允' meaning 'did not permit.'
  • 未幾晴霁 - 'After a short while, the rain stopped and the sky cleared,' with '未幾' meaning 'after a short while' and '晴霁' meaning 'clear and sunny.'
  • 老仆欣然归 - 'The old servant happily returned,' with '欣然' meaning 'happily.'
  • 妻子见之 - 'His wife and children saw him,' with '见之' meaning 'saw him.'
  • 方讶其主之言谬妄 - 'Were just surprised that their master's words were absurd,' with '方讶' meaning 'just surprised' and '谬妄' meaning 'absurd.'
  • 仆曰 - 'The servant said,' with '仆曰' meaning 'the servant said.'
  • 主去后雨即来 - 'The master left, and the rain immediately came,' with '即来' meaning 'immediately came.'
  • 我跪树下，瞑目受死 - 'I knelt under the tree, closed my eyes and accepted my fate,' with '瞑目受死' meaning 'closed my eyes and accepted my fate.'
  • 心无杂念，惟口诵索习之三官经 - 'My mind had no distractions, only reciting the Three Officials Sutra I had memorized,' with '心无杂念' meaning 'mind had no distractions' and '惟口诵' meaning 'only reciting.'
  • 闻雷声下击，及树而回 - 'I heard the thunder strike, and it bounced back upon hitting the tree,' with '及树而回' meaning 'upon hitting the tree and bouncing back.'
  • 我张目视之 - 'I opened my eyes and looked at it,' with '张目视之' meaning 'opened my eyes and looked at it.'
  • 见一大道士高与树倍 - 'I saw a great Taoist master who was twice as tall as the tree,' with '高与树倍' meaning 'twice as tall as the tree.'
  • 身坐树颠，以袍幅垂护 - 'He sat at the top of the tree, with his robe hanging down to protect me,' with '身坐树颠' meaning 'sitting at the top of the tree' and '袍幅垂护' meaning 'robe hanging down to protect.'
  • 我正探首仰观间 - 'I was just looking up when,' with '正探首仰观间' meaning 'just looking up when.'
  • 谅必雷神见我霹雳骤下 - 'I assumed that the Thunder God saw me and the lightning struck down,' with '谅必' meaning 'I assumed' and '霹雳骤下' meaning 'lightning struck down.'
  • 道士挥之以肱，雷始收声 - 'The Taoist master waved his arm, and the thunder stopped,' with '挥之以肱' meaning 'waved his arm' and '雷始收声' meaning 'thunder stopped.'
  • 道士亦不知所往 - 'The Taoist master also disappeared,' with '不知所往' meaning 'disappeared.'
  • 我幸免于难 - 'I was fortunate to escape,' with '幸免于难' meaning 'fortunate to escape.'
  • 将告主人虔诚祀之，以蒙其庥耳 - 'I will inform the master to worship him sincerely, to receive his protection,' with '将告主人' meaning 'I will inform the master,' '虔诚祀之' meaning 'worship him sincerely,' and '以蒙其庥' meaning 'to receive his protection.'
  • 故吾邑崇奉三官菩萨者，至今甚盛 - 'Therefore, the worship of the Three Officials in our county is still very prevalent,' with '故' meaning 'therefore' and '崇奉' meaning 'worship.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 三官经 - The Three Officials Sutra, a text associated with Taoist beliefs in the Three Officials (天官, 地官, 水官), who are believed to have the power to grant blessings and ward off calamities.
  • 雷神 - The Thunder God, a deity in Chinese mythology who controls thunder and lightning.
  • 道士 - A Taoist priest or master, often depicted as having supernatural abilities.
  • 三官菩萨 - The Three Officials, also known as the Three Officials of Heaven, Earth, and Water, who are revered in Taoism.
  • 吾邑 - 'Our county,' referring to the local area where the story takes place.
  • 至今甚盛 - 'Still very prevalent,' indicating the continued popularity of the practice.

Proper Nouns

  • 三官经 - The Three Officials Sutra.
  • 雷神 - The Thunder God.
  • 道士 - Taoist master.
  • 三官菩萨 - The Three Officials.
  • 吾邑 - Our county.

  • 神之功德大矣 - Means 'the divine merit is great indeed', praising the deity's virtues.
  • 天君 - Refers to the supreme deity, often equated with the emperor in imperial cults.
  • 三官 - Refers to the Three Officials, deities in Taoist cosmology.
  • 雷神 - Refers to the god of thunder, often depicted as a powerful and vengeful deity.
  • 帝使戳人 - Means 'the emperor orders someone to be executed'.
  • 憲司 - Refers to the officials responsible for legal and administrative matters.
  • 歷陳其人生平之善 - Means 'to enumerate the person's good deeds throughout life'.
  • 德可掩眚 - Means 'virtue can cover up faults', suggesting that good deeds can atone for bad ones.
  • 帝必赦之 - Means 'the emperor will certainly pardon him'.
  • 使其人凶惡 - Means 'if the person is evil and cruel'.
  • 執奏以為能奉事 - Means 'to present a case stating that the person can serve well'.

  • 福善祸淫 - Means 'blessing the virtuous and punishing the wicked', a principle of divine justice.
  • 彼苍之定理也 - Means 'the fixed law of heaven'.
  • 谄渎何为哉 - Means 'why flatter and show disrespect?'.

  • 温故入闱 - Means 'to review old knowledge and enter the examination hall,' indicating preparation and participation in the exams.
  • 访而录之 - Means 'to visit and copy it,' indicating the act of seeking out and reproducing a document or text.
  • 欣然 - Means 'gladly' or 'joyfully,' indicating a positive and enthusiastic reaction.
  • 发科甲入词林 - Means 'to pass the imperial examinations and enter the literary bureaucracy,' indicating success in the exams and subsequent career advancement.
  • 屡困场屋 - Means 'often failed in the examinations,' indicating repeated failure in the imperial exams.
  • 温故 - Means 'to review old knowledge,' indicating the act of revisiting and studying previously learned material.
  • 入闱 - Means 'to enter the examination hall,' indicating participation in the imperial exams.

[309]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 時年已五旬外矣 - Formal expression meaning 'at the age of over fifty.' The phrase '五旬外' is a way of saying 'over fifty' or 'past fifty.'
  • 功名之念甚切 - Means 'the desire for fame and achievement is very strong.' This is a common expression in classical Chinese literature, reflecting the Confucian ideal of seeking fame and success through scholarly and official achievements.
  • 生訪得之，致其景仰之意 - Means 'the student sought out the scholar and expressed his admiration.' The phrase '致其景仰之意' conveys the act of showing deep respect and admiration.
  • 鄉人皆好之 - A reference to a passage from the *Analects* of Confucius, specifically Chapter 13, Verse 23, which states, '鄉人皆好之，何如？子曰：未可也。君子之德風，小人之德草，草上之風，必偃。' This means 'All the people in the village like him, what do you think? Confucius said: Not necessarily. The virtue of a gentleman is like the wind, the virtue of a small person is like grass, when the wind blows over the grass, it must bend.'
  • 小子與先生居隔百里，未由親炙 - Means 'I live a hundred miles away from the master and have not had the opportunity to learn directly from him.' The phrase '未由親炙' is a humble way of expressing the lack of direct instruction.

[310]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 逢相识者告之 - Encountered a familiar person and told them; used to indicate that the event was shared with acquaintances.
  • 一时传作新闻 - It became a piece of news at the time; suggests that the event gained widespread attention quickly.
  • 是夜试题出果对 - The examination questions were revealed and the results were announced on the same night.
  • 兰陔不胜悔恨 - Lan Gai could not bear the regret and sorrow; expresses deep remorse.
  • 得意之作既被人录去 - His favorite work had already been recorded by someone else; indicates that his best effort was taken by another.
  • 谅天意终身不得售矣 - I suspect that heaven's will is that I will never succeed; expresses a pessimistic outlook on future success.
  • 遂信笔一挥，交卷而出 - He then wrote casually and submitted his paper; describes a carefree and spontaneous act.
  • 二三场为门人苦劝讫事 - He was persuaded by his disciples to continue the exams; indicates that he was encouraged to persevere despite his initial reluctance.
  • 是科竟中 - He ultimately passed the exam; indicates a successful outcome.
  • 兰陔以旧作入见座主 - Lan Gai presented his old work to the examiner; refers to the act of showing his previous work to the exam proctor.
  • 门生薄有微名 - I have a slight reputation as a student; modestly acknowledges his standing.
  • 闱中之作，聊以塞责 - The work I did in the examination hall, just to fulfill the requirement; expresses that the work was done merely to meet the minimum standard.
  • 不堪为多士寓目 - It is not worthy of the eyes of many scholars; indicates that the work is of poor quality.
  • 请以此文易之 - Please allow me to replace it with this one; requests that his previous work be used instead.
  • 座主曰 - The examiner said; introduces the examiner's response.
  • 可 - Yes or agreed; indicates acceptance.
  • 虽然此文若在场中，未必中式 - Although this work, if it were in the examination hall, might not have passed; suggests that the quality of the work was questionable.
  • 阅卷如走马看花 - Reading the papers is like looking at flowers from a galloping horse; a metaphor for the cursory nature of the examiners' review.
  • 气机流走者易于动目 - Works that flow smoothly are easier to catch the eye; indicates that well-written works stand out.
  • 非反复数过，不知其佳处 - One must read it several times to appreciate its merits; emphasizes the depth of the work.
  • 试官有此闲情乎 - Do the examiners have the leisure to do this?; questions the likelihood of such a thorough review.
  • 故无益也 - Therefore, it is of no benefit; concludes that the work would not help in passing the exam.
  • 兰陔悟 - Lan Gai realized; indicates a moment of understanding.
  • 随有《读墨一隅》之选 - He then compiled a selection called *Readings in Calligraphy*; indicates the creation of a new work.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 兰陔 - Refers to a historical figure, possibly a scholar or writer.
  • 座主 - Refers to the examiner or the chief examiner in the imperial examination.
  • 闱中 - Refers to the examination hall or the place where the exams are held.
  • 多士 - Refers to many scholars or candidates taking the exam.
  • 《读墨一隅》 - A title of a work compiled by Lan Gai, likely a collection of writings or reflections on calligraphy or literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 兰陔 - The name of the historical figure.
  • 座主 - The title of the examiner.
  • 《读墨一隅》 - The title of the work compiled by Lan Gai.

[311]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 先是 - Temporal marker meaning 'before this' or 'previously.'
  • 第二人想 - Refers to the expectation of achieving second place in an examination.
  • 整顿衣冠 - Literally 'groom one's attire,' but idiomatically means to prepare oneself formally or ceremoniously.
  • 预备筵宴 - Means 'prepare a banquet' or 'arrange a feast.'
  • 思作新孝廉之乐若登天然 - Figurative expression meaning 'to think of the joy of becoming a new xiao-lian (a scholar-official) as if it were ascending to heaven.'
  • 怨恨之极 - Means 'to be filled with extreme resentment or anger.'
  • 怒其父祖曰 - Means 'to angrily say to his ancestors.'
  • 何为诓骗子孙耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Why did you deceive your descendants?'
  • 欲毁木主 - Means 'to want to destroy the ancestral tablet.'
  • 夜复梦父祖来 - Means 'at night, he dreamt that his ancestors came.'
  • 怒责之曰 - Means 'to angrily scold him and say.'
  • 不肖子无知 - Means 'unworthy son, ignorant.'
  • 此中自有天命 - Means 'there is a heavenly decree in this matter.'
  • 汝若不抄截兰陔之文 - Means 'if you had not plagiarized Lan Gai's work.'
  • 彼必自录 - Means 'he would have written it himself.'
  • 又不得中式矣 - Means 'and would not have passed the examination.'
  • 闱中饭食皆出帑项 - Means 'the food in the examination hall comes from the treasury.'
  • 即为天禄 - Means 'it is a heavenly salary.'
  • 非生时注籍，岂易得哉 - Means 'it is not something easily obtained by being registered at birth.'
  • 汝命中尚有一次 - Means 'you still have one chance in your fate.'
  • 不完总不得安静也 - Means 'you will not be at peace until you complete it.'
  • 公等皆抡元夺魁手 - Means 'you all are capable of winning the top honors.'
  • 我自来领钦赐饭食以了公案耳 - Means 'I am here to receive the imperial banquet and settle the matter.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吴生 - Refers to a scholar or candidate taking the imperial examination.
  • 孝廉 - A title given to scholars who passed the local examinations and were recommended for the imperial examination.
  • 兰陔 - Refers to another scholar or candidate who passed the examination.
  • 木主 - Ancestral tablets used in traditional Chinese ancestor worship.
  • 闱 - The examination hall where the imperial examinations were held.
  • 帑项 - The treasury or public funds.
  • 天禄 - A term for the salary or stipend provided by the state, often associated with the imperial examination system.
  • 抡元夺魁 - Metaphors for winning the top honors in the examination.

Proper Nouns

  • 吴生 - The name of the scholar.
  • 兰陔 - The name of another scholar who passed the examination.
  • 孝廉 - The title given to scholars who passed the local examinations.
  • 木主 - Ancestral tablets.
  • 闱 - The examination hall.
  • 帑项 - The treasury.
  • 天禄 - The imperial stipend.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 北闱大学士某公典试 - Northern examination district, an unspecified official, presiding over the examination.
  • 题为“回也闻一以知十”二句 - The question posed in the examination, a Confucian saying meaning to understand ten things from hearing one.
  • 不慊士心 - Dissatisfy or disappoint the hearts of the scholars.
  • 好事者撰新戏云 - Someone who enjoys creating or spreading news writes a new play or story.
  • 玉帝巡狩 - The Jade Emperor, the supreme deity in Chinese mythology, inspects or tours.
  • 太白星官 - The planet Venus and its deity, often associated with military and strategic matters.
  • 不才而遇，才而不遇者 - Lacking talent but encountering success, talented but not encountering success.
  • 义命 - Righteousness or proper conduct, fate or destiny.
  • 怨毒之气 - Resentment or hatred, atmosphere or aura.
  • 乡试取士，俱有定额 - Provincial examination, selecting scholars, fixed quotas.
  • 二教中仙佛 - Two major religions in Chinese culture, Buddhism and Taoism, immortals and Buddhas.
  • 弄法欺人，兴妖作怪之辈 - Manipulate rules to deceive people, create chaos and mischief.
  • 仿照人闱，举行乡试 - Imitate the human examination system, hold the provincial examination.
  • 文昌历举文理优长之神仙 - God of Literature, list or enumerate, excelling in literature and reason, immortals.
  • 儒童菩萨、文殊菩萨及地下修文郎辈 - Buddhist deity associated with learning, Bodhisattva of wisdom, scholars in the underworld.
  • 玉京尚无典试者 - Capital of the heavenly realm, no one presiding over the examination.
  • 应位尊爵显者为之 - Occupy a position, someone of high rank and prestige, do it.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 北闱 - Northern examination district, significant location for the imperial examinations.
  • 回也闻一以知十 - Confucian saying emphasizing the importance of understanding and learning from limited information.
  • 玉帝 - Jade Emperor, supreme deity in Chinese mythology, associated with the heavens and the afterlife.
  • 太白星官 - Planet Venus and its deity, associated with military and strategic matters.
  • 乡试 - Provincial examination, crucial part of the imperial examination system used to select government officials.
  • 二教 - Two major religions in Chinese culture, Buddhism and Taoism.
  • 文昌 - God of Literature, often invoked for scholarly success and protection.
  • 儒童菩萨、文殊菩萨 - Buddhist deities associated with learning and wisdom.
  • 地下修文郎 - Scholars in the underworld, concept from Chinese folklore and mythology.

Proper Nouns

  • 北闱 - Northern examination district.
  • 回也 - Ran You, a disciple of Confucius.
  • 玉帝 - Jade Emperor.
  • 太白星官 - Planet Venus and its deity.
  • 乡试 - Provincial examination.
  • 文昌 - God of Literature.
  • 儒童菩萨 - Buddhist deity associated with learning.
  • 文殊菩萨 - Bodhisattva of wisdom.
  • 地下修文郎 - Scholars in the underworld.
  • 玉京 - Capital of the heavenly realm.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乃舉齊天大聖孫悟空 - Literal expression meaning 'then selected Sun Wukong, the Great Sage Equal to Heaven.' Introduces the main character, Sun Wukong, a legendary figure in Chinese folklore.
  • 尊矣，顯矣 - Means 'respected and eminent,' indicating the high status of Sun Wukong.
  • 奈其不通文墨乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'but he does not know how to read or write,' highlighting a potential drawback of Sun Wukong's abilities.
  • 天下試官未必盡通 - Means 'officials who administer exams may not all be proficient,' suggesting that not all examiners are knowledgeable.
  • 猴子最靈 - Means 'monkeys are the most clever,' emphasizing Sun Wukong's intelligence.
  • 奉命之後，自能設法延請高明相助 - Means 'after receiving the order, he will surely find ways to invite learned people to assist,' indicating Sun Wukong's resourcefulness.
  • 可無慮矣 - Means 'there is no need to worry,' expressing confidence in Sun Wukong's ability to handle the task.
  • 爰召悟空，命之主試 - Means 'then summoned Sun Wukong and ordered him to preside over the examination,' describing the official's action.
  • 不得推辭 - Means 'he must not refuse,' emphasizing the mandatory nature of the task.
  • 不敢違命 - Means 'dared not disobey,' showing Sun Wukong's obedience.
  • 入文昌宫，请友为助 - Means 'went to the Palace of Literature to ask for friends to help,' indicating the place where Sun Wukong sought assistance.
  • 天聋、地哑二童俱被人重聘去矣 - Means 'the two children, Heaven's Deaf and Earth's Dumb, have already been heavily engaged by others,' explaining why Sun Wukong could not get help from the Palace of Literature.
  • 焉有余人 - Means 'how can there be any others,' emphasizing the lack of available helpers.
  • 吕纯阳系大唐进士，必通文理 - Means 'Lü Chún'yáng, a Jinshi from the Tang Dynasty, must be well-versed in literature,' introducing Lü Chún'yáng and his qualifications.
  • 我已奉命典试琅环福地 - Means 'I have already been ordered to administer the examination in the Land of Immortals,' indicating Lü Chún'yáng's current duties.
  • 何暇相助 - Means 'how can I have time to help,' expressing Lü Chún'yáng's inability to assist.
  • 无已，或访知命之士，以命取人，亦不为屈 - Means 'if there is no other way, perhaps I can find someone fated to help, and it won't be a burden,' suggesting a possible alternative solution.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 齊天大聖孫悟空 - Refers to Sun Wukong, the Great Sage Equal to Heaven, a legendary monkey king in Chinese mythology, known for his magical powers and mischievous nature.
  • 文昌宫 - Refers to the Palace of Literature, a mythical place associated with learning and wisdom.
  • 天聋、地哑 - Refers to Heaven's Deaf and Earth's Dumb, two legendary children in Chinese mythology, often associated with the Palace of Literature.
  • 吕纯阳 - Refers to Lü Dongbin, one of the Eight Immortals in Chinese mythology, known for his wisdom and magical abilities.
  • 大唐进士 - Refers to a Jinshi, a scholar-official in the Tang Dynasty, indicating Lü Chún'yáng's high status and knowledge.
  • 琅环福地 - Refers to the Land of Immortals, a mythical place associated with longevity and immortality.

Proper Nouns

  • 齊天大聖孫悟空 - Sun Wukong, the Great Sage Equal to Heaven.
  • 文昌宫 - Palace of Literature.
  • 天聋、地哑 - Heaven's Deaf and Earth's Dumb.
  • 吕纯阳 - Lü Dongbin.
  • 大唐进士 - Jinshi from the Tang Dynasty.
  • 琅环福地 - Land of Immortals.

[315]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 江右召贡生 - Refers to a scholar from the Jiangxi region who was summoned to take the imperial examination.
  • 业儒 - Means 'to study Confucian classics' or 'to pursue a scholarly career.'
  • 廪膳生 - Refers to a student who receives a government stipend.
  • 增广生 - Refers to a student who has been promoted to a higher rank in the scholar-gentry system.
  • 童试 - Refers to the preliminary examination for the imperial examination.
  • 不售 - Means 'to fail' or 'not to pass.'
  • 老童生 - Refers to a student who has taken the preliminary examination multiple times without success.
  • 谪在厨房司爨 - Means 'demoted to the kitchen to cook.'
  • 烧火三相公 - A colloquial term for the third son, who is demoted to the kitchen.
  • 开科年 - Refers to the year when the imperial examination is held.
  • 高列优等 - Means 'to rank highly' or 'to excel.'
  • 入闱 - Refers to entering the examination hall.
  • 父命难违耳 - Means 'I cannot disobey my father's command.'
  • 何二伯若座上客，汝为灶下养耶 - A rhetorical question meaning 'Why is your eldest brother treated as a guest while you are treated as a kitchen servant?'
  • 丈人 - Refers to one's father-in-law.
  • 方伯管库之吏 - Refers to an official responsible for managing the treasury.
  • 捐纳省监 - Means 'to purchase a position as a student in the provincial academy through financial contribution.'
  • 专司 - Refers to a designated official or position.
  • 金珠在 - Means 'I have some gold and jewels.'
  • 质与丈人 - Means 'pledge them to my father-in-law.'
  • 纳一监生 - Means 'to purchase a position as a student in the academy.'
  • 观光闱屋 - Means 'to participate in the examination.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 江右 - Refers to the Jiangxi region in southern China.
  • 贡生 - Refers to a scholar who has passed the local examination and is eligible to take the imperial examination.
  • 廪膳生 - Refers to a student who receives a government stipend, indicating a higher status within the scholar-gentry system.
  • 增广生 - Refers to a student who has been promoted to a higher rank, indicating a higher level of achievement.
  • 童试 - Refers to the preliminary examination for the imperial examination, which was a crucial step for scholars aspiring to enter government service.
  • 老童生 - Refers to a student who has taken the preliminary examination multiple times without success, often due to the highly competitive nature of the exams.
  • 烧火三相公 - A colloquial term for the third son, who is demoted to the kitchen, indicating a lower social status within the family.
  • 开科年 - Refers to the year when the imperial examination is held, which was a significant event in the lives of scholars.
  • 丈人 - Refers to one's father-in-law, indicating a familial relationship.
  • 方伯 - An official responsible for managing the treasury, indicating a position of authority.
  • 省监 - A provincial academy.
  • 金珠在 - Refers to the possession of gold and jewels, indicating wealth.
  • 质与丈人 - Refers to the act of pledging one's wealth to a family member, indicating a form of financial assistance.
  • 纳一监生 - Refers to the act of purchasing a position as a student in the academy, indicating a way to gain access to education.
  • 观光闱屋 - Refers to the act of participating in the examination, indicating a desire to gain recognition and advancement.

Proper Nouns

  • 江右 - The region of Jiangxi.
  • 贡生 - A scholar who has passed the local examination.
  • 廪膳生 - A student who receives a government stipend.
  • 增广生 - A student who has been promoted to a higher rank.
  • 童试 - The preliminary examination for the imperial examination.
  • 丈人 - The father-in-law.
  • 方伯 - An official responsible for managing the treasury.
  • 省监 - A provincial academy.

[316]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 父曰 - Direct speech marker meaning 'the father said.'
  • 遺才不取 - Refers to a talented person who is not selected or taken into consideration.
  • 或貼出牆東 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'sticking out east of the wall,' but it metaphorically refers to being overlooked or unrecognized.
  • 看汝何言對丈人耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'what will you say to your father-in-law?' This is a challenge to the son, implying that he should be prepared to justify his actions.
  • 及錄遺，公然附取 - '及錄遺' means 'when the list of candidates is recorded,' and '公然附取' means 'openly attached himself to the list.'
  • 得随两兄入闱 - Means 'he was able to enter the examination hall with his two brothers.'
  • 因三相公食量甚宏 - '因' means 'because,' '三相公' refers to the third son (a respectful title), and '食量甚宏' means 'has a very large appetite.'
  • 其父为之备绍酒、金蹄、烧鸭、熏鹅之类 - '其父' means 'his father,' '为之备' means 'prepared for him,' and '绍酒、金蹄、烧鸭、熏鹅' are specific dishes: '绍酒' (Shao wine), '金蹄' (golden trotter), '烧鸭' (roast duck), and '熏鹅' (smoked goose).
  • 三相公领卷入号舍 - '领卷' means 'carrying the exam papers,' and '入号舍' means 'entering the examination room.'
  • 见其左右邻皆武林寒士 - '见' means 'saw,' '其左右邻' means 'his neighbors on both sides,' and '武林寒士' means 'poor scholars from Wulin (West Lake area).'
  • 慷慨食之 - '慷慨' means 'generously,' and '食之' means 'eating them.'
  • 邻士皆悦 - '邻士' means 'the neighboring scholars,' and '皆悦' means 'all were pleased.'
  • 是夜题来，“譬如为山”四句 - '是夜' means 'that night,' '题来' means 'wrote down,' and '譬如为山' is a quote from the *Analects* of Confucius, meaning 'It is like building a mountain.'
  • 至次日之下午 - '至' means 'until,' '次日' means 'the next day,' and '下午' means 'afternoon.'
  • 三相公仍大烹以延邻士 - '仍' means 'still,' '大烹' means 'a grand feast,' and '延' means 'to invite.'
  • 皆议论名人作法 - '皆' means 'all,' '议论' means 'discussed,' '名人' means 'famous people,' and '作法' means 'methods or techniques.'
  • 三相公默然 - '默然' means 'silently' or 'in silence.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 父 - Refers to the father, a common familial role.
  • 丈人 - Refers to the father-in-law, a term of respect.
  • 遗才 - Refers to a talented person who is not selected, often a reference to a scholar who is overlooked.
  • 武林 - Refers to the West Lake area, a famous cultural and educational center.
  • 寒士 - Refers to poor scholars, often used to describe those from humble backgrounds.
  • 号舍 - Refers to the examination room or testing room.
  • 譬如为山 - A quote from the *Analects* of Confucius, emphasizing the importance of perseverance and gradual progress.

Proper Nouns

  • 父 - The father.
  • 丈人 - The father-in-law.
  • 遗才 - A talented person who is not selected.
  • 武林 - The West Lake area.
  • 寒士 - Poor scholars.
  • 号舍 - The examination room.
  • 譬如为山 - A quote from the *Analects* of Confucius.

[317]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鄰士叩其故 - Classical phrase meaning 'the neighbor asked the reason.'
  • 初次观光 - Literally 'first time to see the capital,' referring to taking the imperial examination.
  • 遇大题敷衍难成 - Means 'encountered a difficult topic that was hard to develop.'
  • 奈何 - An interjection meaning 'what should be done?'
  • 若欲完篇，何难之有 - A rhetorical question meaning 'if you want to complete the essay, what difficulty is there?'
  • 十三经中，不乏山字语头 - Means 'in the Thirteen Classics, there are no shortage of phrases starting with 'mountain.'
  • 莫管义理 - Means 'do not worry about the meaning or logic.'
  • 填砌成文 - Means 'to fill and construct the text.'
  • 洋洋数千言 - Means 'thousands of words, flowing abundantly.'
  • 引用不完 - Means 'cannot be fully cited.'
  • 腹内空虚之故 - Means 'the reason for an empty mind.'
  • 争为写书 - Means 'strive to write the book.'
  • 且教以运用之法 - Means 'and teach the method of application.'
  • 大悟 - Means 'great enlightenment.'
  • 挥洒自如 - Means 'to write freely and effortlessly.'
  • 千言立就 - Means 'a thousand words completed immediately.'
  • 试毕 - Means 'after the exam.'
  • 其长次二兄皆录文呈父 - Means 'the eldest and second brother both copied the text and presented it to their father.'
  • 父乃举酒阅文 - Means 'the father then lifted his wine and read the text.'
  • 恬吟密咏 - Means 'to read softly and intently.'
  • 推为必售之作 - Means 'to recommend it as a sure success.'
  • 技痒 - Means 'itching to show off one's skills.'
  • 以其稿恭呈父前 - Means 'he respectfully presented his draft to his father.'
  • 拍案大呼 - Means 'to bang the table and shout.'
  • 浮泛至此 - Means 'so superficial.'
  • 亦可以见人乎 - Means 'can it be shown to others?'
  • 不知愧恧 - Means 'does not know shame.'
  • 至汝极矣 - Means 'you are the worst.'
  • 碎其文 - Means 'to tear up the text.'
  • 喝令速退 - Means 'to order to leave quickly.'
  • 毋触父怒也 - Means 'do not provoke the father's anger.'
  • 抱头鼠窜而泣 - Means 'to run away like a rat and cry.'

  • 寡人二十余岁时，忽得眩疾，发则眩晕即死，过日而苏 - Formal expression meaning 'When I was in my twenties, I suddenly contracted a dizziness disease, and when it struck, I would faint and almost die, but would recover after a few days.'
  • 前王命太史占之，须乞丐中华沿海之区，必有奇遇，疾以痊也 - Formal expression meaning 'The previous king ordered the historian to divinate, and it was said that I must beg in the coastal regions of China, and there would be a strange encounter that would cure the disease.'
  • 寡人带渠行乞，得遇先生以仙丹救治，寡人自此疾愈，逮今十余年不复发矣 - Formal expression meaning 'I took them with me on my begging journey, and I met the master who treated me with elixir, and from then on, my illness was cured, and I have not had it again for over ten years.'
  • 昔年王父崩，寡人接位，国服甫满，尚未朝贡请封，故国人犹以小王子称寡人耳 - Formal expression meaning 'Last year, when my father passed away, I ascended the throne, and the mourning period was just over, and we had not yet paid tribute and requested investiture, so the people still call me the young prince.'
  • 今天幸先生来此，俾寡人有以报德，先生欲贵乎？富乎？欲贵，则特设宾师之位以奉先生；欲富，则珍宝货财惟先生命 - Formal expression meaning 'Today, I am fortunate that the master has come here, allowing me to repay your kindness. Do you desire nobility or wealth? If you desire nobility, I will specially set up a position of guest teacher to honor you; if you desire wealth, I will give you treasures and wealth as you wish.'

  • 故知其受托不苟也 - Concludes that this person is trustworthy and does not act carelessly.
  • 陬邑官亲 - Refers to a relative of a local official (陬邑官).
  • 西域之变 - Refers to a significant event or conflict in the Western Regions (西域).
  • 大将军福公 - Refers to a high-ranking military official (大将军) named Fu (福).
  • 统领巴图鲁及吉林健旅 - Describes the general leading a force of elite soldiers (巴图鲁) and troops from Jilin (吉林健旅).
  • 声势赫奕 - Means 'with great prestige and influence.'
  • 传檄至陬邑 - Refers to an official order or proclamation sent to the local area (陬邑).
  • 缺既清苦 - Describes the local area as poor and underdeveloped.
  • 官亦疲惫 - Indicates that the local officials are exhausted or overworked.
  • 闻前途才干之员每有失误 - Means that officials who are skilled or capable often make mistakes.
  • 心切恐惶 - Describes a state of extreme anxiety or fear.
  • 日惟涕泣而已 - Means 'he could only cry every day.'
  • 默默无闻 - Describes someone who is unknown or unremarkable.
  • 不忍坐视 - Means 'unable to stand by and do nothing.'
  • 库中有二三百金乎 - A question asking if there is two or three hundred gold coins in the treasury.
  • 不敷所用 - Means 'not enough for the purpose.'
  • 既有之，尚可为也 - A conditional statement meaning 'if there is, it can still be done.'
  • 尽以予我 - Means 'give it all to me.'
  • 不问出入 - Indicates that the old man will not ask about the details of how the money is used.
  • 或者无碍前程乎 - A speculative question meaning 'perhaps it won't harm your future?'
  • 时值盛署炎炎 - Describes a period of intense summer heat.
  • 几至流金烁石 - A hyperbolic expression meaning 'the heat is so intense that it could melt metal and burn stone.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 进士 - Refers to someone who has passed the imperial examination, a highly respected status in Chinese society.
  • 巴图鲁 - A title given to elite soldiers in the Qing dynasty, indicating bravery and military prowess.
  • 吉林健旅 - Refers to troops from Jilin, a region in northeastern China known for its military contributions.
  • 西域 - Refers to the Western Regions, a broad term for areas west of China, often associated with Central Asia.
  • 陬邑 - Refers to a specific locality, likely a small town or county in China.
  • 大将军 - A high-ranking military title in Chinese history.
  • 传檄 - Refers to an official order or proclamation sent by a military or administrative authority.

  • 俾屋上棚间不干不湿，泽润而已 - To make the roof and ceiling neither dry nor wet, moist and lustrous.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 或日 - Classical expression meaning 'some say' or 'there is a saying,' introducing a quote or opinion.
  • 才能之士湮没无闻 - Literally 'talented individuals are buried without fame,' meaning talented people are overlooked or forgotten.
  • 惜乎 - Expression of regret or pity, equivalent to 'what a pity!' or 'how unfortunate!'
  • 此其小焉者耳 - Means 'this is just a minor aspect of it,' downplaying the issue being discussed.
  • 古来救君主于垂危 - Literally 'from ancient times, saving the sovereign in dire straits,' referring to historical figures who saved their rulers in critical situations.
  • 转覆败于俄顷 - Literally 'turning defeat into victory in a moment,' meaning reversing a situation from failure to success in a short time.
  • 史不列其姓名者 - Means 'those whose names are not listed in history,' referring to individuals who made significant contributions but are not recorded.
  • 如赵之厮养卒，金之两书生 - Refers to specific historical figures: 'the servant of Zhao' and 'the two scholars of Jin,' who are examples of individuals who made significant contributions but are not well-known.
  • 功高不赏 - Means 'high achievement not rewarded,' referring to individuals who performed great deeds but did not receive recognition.
  • 是以名没不彰 - Means 'thus their names are not made known,' explaining why their contributions are forgotten.
  • 其怨恸于九原耶 - Literally 'their resentment is profound in the netherworld,' suggesting that these individuals might be deeply resentful in the afterlife.
  • 抑或不屑不洁 - Means 'or perhaps they disdain impurity,' suggesting that these individuals might have chosen to remain anonymous due to a sense of moral purity.
  • 如遗逸辈之特隐其名耶 - Means 'like the recluses who particularly hide their names,' referring to individuals who chose to remain anonymous.
  • 东坡云 - Refers to a saying by Su Dongpo (Su Shi), a famous Song Dynasty poet and scholar.
  • 但愿我儿愚且鲁 - Means 'I only wish my son to be stupid and dull,' expressing a preference for a simple and unambitious life.
  • 无灾无害到公卿 - Means 'without disaster or harm, reaching the position of a high official,' suggesting a life free from trouble and leading to a high rank.
  • 由此观之 - Means 'from this perspective,' introducing a conclusion based on the preceding statements.
  • 是卒与书生固不屑为公卿也 - Means 'thus the servants and scholars indeed disdain to become high officials,' concluding that these individuals preferred a simpler life over high office.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 才能之士 - Refers to talented individuals.
  • 赵之厮养卒 - Refers to a servant of the state of Zhao, a historical figure who saved his ruler in a critical situation.
  • 金之两书生 - Refers to two scholars of the state of Jin, who also made significant contributions but are not well-known.
  • 东坡 - Refers to Su Dongpo (Su Shi), a famous Song Dynasty poet and scholar.
  • 公卿 - Refers to high-ranking officials or nobles.
  • 九原 - Refers to the netherworld or the afterlife.
  • 遗逸辈 - Refers to recluses or individuals who choose to live in seclusion.

Proper Nouns

  • 东坡 - Su Dongpo (Su Shi), a famous Song Dynasty poet and scholar.
  • 公卿 - High-ranking officials or nobles.
  • 九原 - The netherworld or the afterlife.
  • 遗逸辈 - Recluses or individuals who choose to live in seclusion.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 同胞三鼎甲 - Three siblings achieving the highest ranks in the imperial examination system.
  • 明季之乱 - The turmoil of the late Ming dynasty, leading up to its fall.
  • 流贼之暴 - The violence and banditry of the time, particularly during the late Ming period.
  • 伪大将军 - A pretender or rebel claiming the title of general.
  • 伪王调取进兵 - A false king (rebel leader) calling for troops to advance.
  • 寄存翁家 - To entrust or leave the women with the old man's household.
  • 闲房宽大 - Spacious, unused rooms in the old man's home, suitable for housing the women.
  • 养赡 - To provide for or support, indicating the old man's generosity.
  • 遐迩称善人 - Far and near, people call him a good person, indicating the old man's widespread respect.
  • 戕官劫库 - Killing officials and looting treasuries, common during the late Ming period of unrest.
  • 圣主即真 - The true sovereign ascended the throne, referring to the establishment of the Qing dynasty after the fall of the Ming.
  • 戡定大难 - To quell or suppress a great calamity, referring to the Qing's suppression of the Ming loyalists and rebels.
  • 复业 - To resume one's former occupation or livelihood, indicating people returning to normal life after the chaos.
  • 读书入泮 - To study and enter the school of learning, referring to the imperial examination system where successful candidates would be admitted to the scholar-official class.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 明季之乱 - Refers to the late Ming dynasty period of political instability, economic hardship, and social unrest, which eventually led to the fall of the Ming and the rise of the Qing dynasty.
  • 流贼 - Refers to the banditry and violence of the time, particularly the activities of the rebel forces during the late Ming period, such as the Li Zicheng rebellion.
  • 伪大将军 - Refers to a pretender or rebel claiming the title of general, likely a leader of one of the rebel factions, such as Li Zicheng or other regional warlords.
  • 伪王 - Refers to a false king or rebel leader, likely one of the many pretenders who emerged during the late Ming period.
  • 圣主即真 - Refers to the establishment of the Qing dynasty after the fall of the Ming, specifically the ascension of the Shunzhi Emperor in 1644.
  • 读书入泮 - Refers to the imperial examination system, where successful candidates would be admitted to the scholar-official class, a key institution in Chinese society during the Ming and Qing dynasties.

Proper Nouns

  • 明季 - Refers to the late Ming dynasty.
  • 圣主 - Refers to the ruler of the Qing dynasty, specifically the Shunzhi Emperor.
  • 三鼎甲 - Refers to the top three ranks in the imperial examination system, indicating that the three sons of the old man achieved high positions in the scholar-official class.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 欲于中门悬匾额 - Describes his desire for public recognition, indicating he wanted to hang a plaque on the main gate.

  • 是不能越分 - Indicates a sense of propriety, suggesting this cannot go beyond his proper place.

  • 命悬于门右 - Describes her decision, indicating she ordered it to be hung on the right side of the gate.
  • 众皆窃笑之 - Describes the reaction of others, indicating everyone secretly laughed at him.
  • 然不敢不从 - Indicates a sense of respect and obedience, suggesting they dared not disobey.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 是時 - Temporal phrase meaning 'at that time' or 'during that period.'
  • 猶諸生也 - Means 'still a student' or 'yet a scholar,' indicating the protagonist's current status.
  • 兩弟皆飛騰而去 - Metaphorical expression where '飛騰' means 'to rise to prominence' or 'to achieve success,' and '而去' means 'to depart' or 'to leave.'
  • 我恐投世無聞矣 - Compound phrase where '投世' means 'to enter the world' or 'to engage with society,' and '無聞' means 'to remain unknown' or 'to achieve no fame.'
  • 偏師先濟 - Military metaphor where '偏師' means 'a smaller force' or 'a detachment,' and '先濟' means 'to succeed first' or 'to achieve success ahead of others.'
  • 妾预言之 - Means 'I predicted it' or 'I foretold it,' with '妾' being a modest term for 'I' used by a woman.
  • 郎君毋灰厥志 - Phrase where '郎君' means 'young master' or 'young man,' '毋' means 'do not,' '灰' means 'to lose hope,' and '厥志' means 'your aspirations.'
  • 龙头属老成 - Metaphor where '龙头' means 'the lead' or 'the top,' and '老成' means 'experienced' or 'mature.'
  • 俟元气充沛 - Means 'awaiting the fullness of one's energy' or 'waiting until one's strength is complete.'
  • 自当超两弟而上之 - Means 'will naturally surpass the two younger brothers' or 'will naturally rise above them.'
  • 仅登榜尾 - Means 'barely made the list' or 'just managed to pass.'
  • 越三年 - Means 'after three years' or 'three years later.'
  • 果状元及第 - Means 'indeed became the top scholar' or 'actually passed as the top candidate.'
  • 泥金报至 - Means 'the gold-embossed notice arrived' or 'the official announcement came.'
  • 戚里无不佩服夫人者 - Means 'the relatives and neighbors all admired the wife' or 'the family and community all respected the wife.'
  • 尽善尽美 - Means 'perfectly complete' or 'without flaw.'
  • 文笔之发与不发 - Means 'the flourishing or lack of flourishing of one's writing' or 'the success or failure of one's literary career.'
  • 两叔鼎甲 - Means 'the two uncles who achieved the highest ranks' or 'the two uncles who passed as top scholars.'
  • 何能预定传胪耶 - Means 'how can one predict the runner-up' or 'how can one foresee the second place.'
  • 姑请试之 - Means 'let us try it' or 'let us see if it works.'
  • 议开文会 - Means 'to hold a literary gathering' or 'to organize a literary event.'
  • 凡邑中之生童未婚者皆延之 - Means 'all unmarried young scholars in the county were invited' or 'all unmarried students in the county were invited.'
  • 勿论贫富 - Means 'regardless of wealth or poverty' or 'without regard to financial status.'
  • 三复而得一生 - Means 'after multiple rounds, one person was chosen' or 'after several attempts, a single individual was selected.'
  • 甚寒苦 - Means 'very poor and miserable' or 'extremely destitute.'
  • 使王夫人督课之 - Means 'have the wife of Wang supervise his studies' or 'have Wang's wife oversee his education.'
  • 传胪及第 - Means 'passed as the runner-up' or 'achieved second place.'

  • 此为恶之报也 - Literally 'this is the retribution for evil,' a moral judgment about the consequences of the man's actions.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sequence of sentences describes the retrieval of a weapon, the preparation to shoot the snake, and the investigation that found no trace.
  • This sequence of sentences describes the person pointing to the snake, preparing to shoot, and the resulting loud shot and severe injury.
  • This sequence of sentences describes the aftermath of the incident, including the damage to the window and the gun, and the person's account of the event.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '吾乡有朱氏翁，年周甲，为米市伙' introduces the main character and his background.
  • The clause '其为人也，严以正己，和以接物，故人皆亲之' describes the old man's character and its effect on others.
  • The clause '每出行，遇有碍足之物，必去净而后已' describes the old man's habitual behavior.
  • The clause '见棺椁之暴露者，必为掩盖' describes another aspect of the old man's behavior.
  • The clause '一日索逋至野，见破冢内有巨瓮，白镪满中' describes the old man's discovery of the jar.
  • The clause '翁恐且迷，方捡阅间，冢旁农人觏之奔而前曰' describes the old man's reaction and the farmer's accusation.
  • The clause '此我祖父墓也，方因雨破。汝徘徊其间，得无盗我墓中物耶' describes the farmer's accusation.
  • The clause '翁谢过曰：“原物归君，我未动毫厘也。”' describes the old man's response to the accusation.
  • The clause '农人识翁，故挥之去。而呼其兄弟子侄来曰' describes the farmer's reaction and his next action.
  • The clause '冢中不知谁氏物露我目中，天其富我乎，盍共取之' describes the farmer's curiosity and greed.
  • The clause '众皆合力起出，视瓮中盘旋蠕动皆毒蛇也' describes the group's discovery of the snakes.
  • The clause '农人恚曰：“翁先见此，而曰原物，戏我实甚，我其还戏之。”' describes the farmer's anger and his plan for revenge.
  • The clause '共舁瓮至翁宅后，俟其寝息，升屋拨瓦而倾之' describes the farmer's prank.
  • The clause '翁夫妇跃起曰：“天雨金矣，姑趋避之。”' describes the old man's and his wife's reaction.
  • The clause '候雨定而后捡较，得数千金，家以是富' describes the outcome of the prank.
  • The clause '彼农人倾毕，负空瓮归，方自以为得计也' describes the farmer's final action and his satisfaction.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 眾僧皆喜公和婉 - The monks all liked [his] amiable and gentle nature. *和婉* is a classical term meaning 'gentle and amiable,' often used to describe a person's demeanor or character.
  • 暇即聚談 - 'Whenever they had leisure, they gathered to talk.' *暇* refers to free time or leisure, and *聚談* means 'to gather and converse.'
  • 日習見先生書法甚佳 - 'They daily became accustomed to seeing [his] excellent handwriting.' *日習見* means 'frequently saw' or 'became accustomed to seeing daily.'
  • 曷不於廟外售聯 - 'Why not sell couplets outside the temple?' *曷不* is a classical way of saying 'why not?'
  • 則所需有着 - 'Then the required funds would be available.' *有着* is a classical expression meaning 'available' or 'available to be obtained.'
  • 買者源源而來 - 'Buyers came in a steady stream.' *源源而來* is an idiom meaning 'to come continuously' or 'in a steady stream.'
  • 春服藉以告成 - 'Spring clothes were thus completed.' *藉以* means 'by means of' or 'through which'; *告成* means 'completed' or 'finished.'
  • 皇太后許書妙法蓮華經百部 - 'The Empress Dowager permitted the copying of one hundred copies of the *Lotus Sutra*.' *許書* means 'allowed to be written' or 'permitted to copy.'
  • 施舍天下名山 - 'To be distributed to famous mountains throughout the land.' *施舍* here is used in a religious or charitable sense, meaning 'to distribute' or 'to give out.'
  • 作大功德 - 'To perform a great meritorious deed.' *功德* is a Buddhist term meaning 'merit' or 'good deeds.'
  • 上命詞林書式呈覽 - 'The Emperor ordered the scholars of the Hanlin Academy to submit samples of writing for review.' *詞林* is a term for the Hanlin Academy or the literary scholars in the imperial court.
  • 不合太后意 - 'Did not meet the Empress Dowager’s approval.' *不合* means 'not in accordance with' or 'did not conform to.'
  • 有旨，旋命諸王在外訪善書者 - 'An imperial decree was issued, and soon the princes were ordered to search outside the capital for skilled calligraphers.' *有旨* means 'an imperial decree was issued'; *旋命* means 'soon ordered.'
  • 王識大和尚 - 'The prince recognized the great monk.' *識* here is used in the sense of 'recognized' or 'knew of.'
  • 命书记僧作字进 - 'Ordered the monk who was in charge of records to write and present it.' *书记僧* is a monk responsible for record-keeping or scriptural matters.
  • 愈不合旨 - 'Even more so did it not meet the imperial will.' *愈* means 'even more'; *不合旨* means 'not in accordance with the emperor’s will.'
  • 惟有責重大和尚而已 - 'There was nothing else to do but blame the great monk.' *惟有* means 'there was only'; *責* means 'to blame' or 'to hold responsible.'
  • 乃与众僧谋，一僧以公对 - 'Then they consulted with the monks, and one monk suggested [him].' *以公对* means 'to recommend [him] as the candidate.'
  • 大和尚晒曰 - 'The great monk scoffed and said.' *晒* is a colloquial term meaning 'to scoff' or 'to mock.'
  • 此不過賣春聯之字法耳 - 'This is merely the style of writing used for selling spring couplets.' *字法* refers to the method or style of writing/calligraphy.
  • 何足以當大任 - 'How could this be sufficient to bear such a great responsibility?' *當大任* means 'to bear a great responsibility.'
  • 盍姑試之 - 'Why not try him for a while?' *盍* is a classical particle meaning 'why not'; *姑試之* means 'try him for a while.'
  • 因王進呈 - 'Through the prince, it was presented to the Empress Dowager.' *進呈* means 'to present to the court' or 'to submit to the emperor.'
  • 太后大悦 - 'The Empress Dowager was greatly pleased.' *大悦* is a classical expression meaning 'greatly pleased.'
  • 命延入王府 - 'Ordered him to be invited into the prince’s residence.' *延* means 'to invite' or 'to summon.'
  • 虔誠書經 - 'To write the scriptures with sincerity and devotion.' *虔誠* is a classical term for 'sincere' or 'devout.'
  • 功成之日，厚酬之 - 'On the day the work was completed, he was richly rewarded.' *厚酬* means 'richly rewarded.'
  • 王自来延请 - 'The prince himself came to invite him.' *自来* means 'personally' or 'himself.'
  • 皇然失措 - 'The great monk was greatly startled and at a loss.' *皇然* is an intensifier meaning 'greatly' or 'in a state of alarm'; *失措* means 'at a loss.'
  • 盛備行裝 - 'Prepared elaborate travel arrangements.' *盛備* means 'carefully prepared' or 'fully prepared.'
  • 書經告竣 - 'The scripture copying was completed.' *告竣* means 'completed' or 'finished.'
  • 上请问合旨之意 - 'The Emperor asked for the meaning behind the approval.' *合旨* means 'in accordance with the imperial will.'
  • 非无书法出其右者 - 'There were no calligraphers whose skill surpassed him.' *出其右* is an idiom meaning 'to surpass' or 'to be better than.'
  • 第福分屆滿，即无功德 - 'But their fortune was complete, so they had no merit.' *第* is used here to mean 'but' or 'however'; *福分屆滿* means 'their fortune was complete' or 'their good karma was exhausted.'
  • 此人将来必为正直大臣，有功于国 - 'This person will certainly become an upright minister and serve the state with merit.' *正直大臣* is a classical term for 'upright minister' or 'honest official.'
  • 其书浑厚多力 - 'His calligraphy is thick, solid, and full of power.' *浑厚* means 'thick, solid, or substantial'; *多力* means 'full of strength.'
  • 可与一官酬之 - 'It is fitting to bestow an official position upon him.' *酬之* means 'to reward' or 'to repay.'
  • 钦赐举人，以部曹用 - 'He was granted the title of *juren* by imperial decree, and was appointed as a *bugao* official.' *钦赐* means 'granted by imperial decree'; *举人* is a classical examination title; *部曹* refers to a bureaucratic official in the central government.
  • 遇缺即补 - 'Whenever a vacancy occurred, he was immediately appointed.' *遇缺* means 'when a vacancy occurred'; *即补* means 'immediately filled.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 眾僧 - Refers to members of a Buddhist temple, likely in the context of a monastery or temple community.
  • 春聯 - Traditional Chinese New Year decorations, typically written on red paper and hung on doors to bring good fortune.
  • 妙法莲华经 - *The Lotus Sutra* is one of the most important Mahayana Buddhist scriptures, central to East Asian Buddhism.
  • 皇太后 - Refers to the mother of the emperor or a ruling female figure in the imperial court.
  • 上 - The Emperor, the reigning monarch, the highest authority in the state.
  • 詞林 - The Hanlin Academy, a prestigious literary and scholarly institution of the imperial court, responsible for drafting official documents, poetry, and calligraphy.
  • 諸王 - Members of the royal family, particularly the sons of the emperor or other high-ranking nobles.
  • 大和尚 - A senior Buddhist monk, possibly the abbot of the temple or a respected spiritual figure.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 在其境 - Within its territory or in the region, indicating the location of the actions.
  • 开水田 - Open up paddy fields; may need contextual adaptation to convey agricultural development.
  • 通河渠 - Connect rivers and canals; refers to improving waterways for irrigation or transportation.
  • 设桥梁 - Build bridges; straightforward translation but may need clarification in context.
  • 初北人不习种棉 - Initially, northerners were unfamiliar with cotton cultivation; may need adaptation for clarity.
  • 公聘南方男妇导之 - The official hired men and women from the south to guide them; '导之' means 'to guide them' or 'to teach them'.
  • 作耕织图以进 - Created illustrations of farming and weaving to present to the emperor; '以进' means 'to present' or 'to offer'.
  • 上为题咏 - The emperor composed verses in response; '题咏' means 'to compose verses' or 'to write poetry'.
  • 知吏之利弊 - Understood the advantages and disadvantages of the officials; literal translation may need adaptation for clarity.
  • 故宫文书皆刻颁格式 - All official documents were engraved and distributed in a standardized format; '刻颁' means 'engraved and distributed'.
  • 使吏不能高下其手 - Ensured that officials could not manipulate or abuse their power; '高下其手' is an idiomatic expression meaning 'to manipulate or abuse power'.
  • 修复义仓 - Repaired the public granaries; '义仓' refers to public granaries used for famine relief.
  • 作义仓图 - Created illustrations of the public granaries; '图' means 'illustration' or 'diagram'.
  • 均贮四乡，以便赈济 - Distributed them evenly across the four villages to facilitate relief efforts; '赈济' means 'relief efforts' or 'famine relief'.
  • 上下肃清 - The hierarchy was clear and orderly; '肃清' means 'clear and orderly'.
  • 闾阊丰足 - The people in the villages were prosperous; '闾阊' refers to villages or neighborhoods.
  • 上益重之 - The emperor valued him even more; '益重' means 'valued even more'.
  • 入觐 - Presented himself to the emperor; '觐' means 'to present oneself to the emperor'.
  • 询公尚未有子之故 - Asked the reason why the official had no sons yet; '询' means 'to ask' and '尚未有子' means 'had no sons yet'.
  • 公以实情奏 - The official reported the truth; '实情' means 'the truth' or 'the facts'.
  • 上为降旨召夫人 - The emperor issued an edict to summon the official's wife; '降旨' means 'issued an edict'.
  • 不得不来 - She had no choice but to come; literal translation may need adaptation for clarity.
  • 以命服迎入署 - She was welcomed into the official's residence in imperial attire; '命服' means 'imperial attire' and '署' means 'residence' or 'office'.
  • 夫人见公再拜曰 - The wife bowed twice to the official and said; '再拜' means 'bowed twice' as a sign of respect.
  • 妾来应君命 - I came in response to your command; '妾' is a humble term for 'I' used by women.
  • 然马齿长矣，无生育理 - However, I am past childbearing age; '马齿长矣' is an idiomatic expression meaning 'I am old' or 'I am past childbearing age'.
  • 仅能主内政而已 - I can only manage the household affairs; '内政' means 'household affairs'.
  • 请纳如姬 - Please take in a concubine like Ruhui; '如姬' refers to a concubine.
  • 公不听 - The official did not listen; literal translation may need adaptation for clarity.
  • 上知之 - The emperor knew about it; '知之' means 'knew about it'.
  • 以江南织造所进宫人赐之 - The emperor granted him a palace woman from the Jiangnan textile factory; '织造' refers to the textile factory and '宫人' means 'palace woman'.
  • 生一子 - He gave birth to a son; '生' means 'gave birth to'.
  • 上贤夫人之不妒 - The emperor praised the wife for not being jealous; '贤' means 'praised' or 'commended'.
  • 加公宫保衔 - Granted the official the title of Palace Guard; '宫保' is a title.
  • 命正奏为一品夫人 - Ordered that she be officially designated as a First-Rank Lady; '一品夫人' means 'First-Rank Lady'.
  • 妾赐宜人 - Granted her concubines the title of Yiren; '宜人' is a title for concubines.
  • 其子后亦为大中丞 - His son later became a Grand Censor; '大中丞' is a high-ranking official title.
  • 芗厈曰 - Xiang Yan said; '芗厈' is the name of the speaker.
  • 公之善政，所以结主知者，当不止此 - The official's good governance, which won the emperor's favor, was certainly not limited to these things; '结主知' means 'won the emperor's favor'.
  • 不过依客所能言者记之 - But it is recorded here based on what the guest could say; '客' refers to the author or narrator.
  • 或曰 - Some say; common introductory phrase for a quote or opinion.
  • 此方宫保讳观承事 - This official's title of Palace Guard was originally given to Guan Chengshi; '讳' means 'originally given to'.
  • 然核公行状及袁简斋先生《随园文集》所载不合 - However, when cross-referenced with the official's biography and Mr. Yuan Jianzhi's *Sui Yuan Wen Ji*, it does not match; '行状' means 'biography' and '不合' means 'does not match'.
  • 意或鄙俗之事，节去以成体裁 - Perhaps it was a matter of low taste or vulgarity; '鄙俗' means 'low taste' or 'vulgarity'.
  • 节去以成体裁 - Omitted to maintain the proper form; '节去' means 'omitted' and '体裁' means 'form' or 'style'.
  • 或传述失实 - Perhaps the transmission was inaccurate; '传述' means 'transmission'.
  • 或未可定 - Or it may not be certain; '未可定' means 'may not be certain'.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 豫人 - A term meaning 'from Yü (豫) province' or more generally 'a person from the region of Yü,' which in classical Chinese often refers to a person from the central plains or Henan region.
  • 前明诸生 - Refers to someone who was a scholar-official (a *sheng*) during the Ming dynasty before it fell. *诸生* is a general term for those who passed the initial level of the imperial examination.
  • 生农家 - 'Born in a farming family,' indicating the protagonist’s humble origins.
  • 性喜水 - 'Loves water by nature,' describing an innate or strong affinity for water.
  • 嬉戏井旁 - 'Playing by the well,' indicating a child’s playful behavior.
  • 照见己影 - 'Saw his own reflection,' a literal description of the child seeing his image in the water.
  • 跃入从之 - 'Jumped in to follow it,' indicating the child’s instinctive reaction to his reflection.
  • 人见其不沈也 - 'People saw that he did not sink,' indicating the miraculous or supernatural nature of the event.
  • 共异之 - 'All found it strange,' meaning they were amazed or astonished.
  • 以绳悬入下 - 'Lowered with a rope into the well,' describing the method used to rescue the child.
  • 抱之起 - 'Carried him up,' indicating the successful rescue.
  • 母夫人又殁 - 'His mother, the lady, died,' a formal way of saying the mother passed away.
  • 其姑渔家妇也 - 'His grandmother was a fisher’s wife,' indicating the grandmother’s occupation and background.
  • 养为己子 - 'Raised him as her own son,' showing the grandmother’s role as a caretaker.
  • 随姑父母舟中 - 'Traveled with his grandmother and grandfather’s boat,' describing the child’s life aboard the boat.
  • 更以弄水为乐 - 'Found even more joy in playing with water,' indicating the child’s increasing fondness for water.
  • 顽梗如故 - 'Still stubborn and unyielding,' describing the child’s unchanging behavior.
  • 厌之 - 'Grew tired of him,' indicating the grandmother’s husband’s frustration.
  • 几激水湿其衣 - 'Almost got his clothes wet with splashing water,' describing an incident that caused the grandfather to become angry.
  • 以足蹬儿 - 'Kicked the child with his foot,' a violent act.
  • 坠河中，随波逐流而去 - 'Fell into the river and was carried away by the current,' indicating the child’s fall into the river.
  • 号泣不已 - 'Cried and wailed without ceasing,' showing the grandmother’s intense grief.
  • 顽童无用，将贻后患，不如早绝之 - 'This mischievous child is useless and will bring future trouble; it would be better to get rid of him early,' a harsh statement from the grandfather.
  • 口角时 - 'During their argument,' indicating the time when the couple was fighting.
  • 下游一舟来 - 'A boat came from downstream,' introducing a new character or event.
  • 无伤也 - 'No harm was done,' a statement from the person on the boat.
  • 儿戏水中，乐甚，无乃是耶？ - 'A child is playing in the water, having great fun—could it be this one?' a question from the boat’s occupant.
  • 果见儿在水中，抱大鱼出入波间 - 'Indeed, they saw the child in the water, holding a large fish as he moved through the waves,' a miraculous image.
  • 如获至宝 - 'As if finding a treasure,' indicating the grandmother’s joy at finding the child.
  • 扶之登舟而回 - 'Helped him onto the boat and returned,' describing the rescue.
  • 知此儿之不能容于其夫也 - 'Knew that this child could not be tolerated by her husband,' indicating the grandmother’s realization of her husband’s hatred.
  • 使受雇为人牧儿 - 'Sent him to work as a hired child caregiver,' indicating the grandmother’s decision to send the child away.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 前明 - Refers to the Ming dynasty before its fall, indicating the time period in which the protagonist was a scholar-official.
  • 诸生 - A term used in the imperial examination system to denote those who had passed the county-level examination and were eligible for further advancement.
  • 豫人 - 'From Yü (豫),' which refers to a region in central China, often associated with Henan Province.
  • 渔家妇 - 'A fisher’s wife,' indicating a common occupation among rural women.
  • 牧儿 - 'Child caregiver' or 'child herder,' a term for a hired person who tends to children, often in a household or estate setting.
  • The narrative reflects traditional Chinese family structure, where a grandmother (姑) might take on the role of a caretaker when the mother is absent or deceased.
  • The repeated emphasis on water and the child’s relationship with it may be symbolic, reflecting themes of destiny, fate, and the supernatural in classical Chinese literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 黄大王 - A proper noun, possibly a nickname or title for the protagonist. The name '黄' (Huang) is a common surname in Chinese, and '大王' (Dàwáng) means 'Great King' or 'Lord,' which may be a title or a term of respect. The exact meaning is uncertain.
  • 豫 - A region in ancient China, often associated with modern-day Henan Province.

Cultural and Historical Allusions
